app, scroll down until you find the "Accessibility" option. Tap on it to proceed. Accessibility settings contain options to adjust features designed to make your iPhone easier to use, such as larger text, voice control, and hearing aid compatibility. Step 3: Select Audio/Visual Under the "HEARING" section of the accessibility settings, tap on the "Audio/Visual" option. This will take you to the page where you can manage audio and visual related settings, including hearing aid mode. Step 4: Locate the Hearing Aid Mode Option On the Audio/Visual settings page, look for the "Hearing Aid Mode" option. It should be located under the "AUDIO" section. If you see a toggle switch next to "Hearing Aid Mode," it means the feature is currently enabled. Step 5: Turn Off Hearing Aid Mode To turn off hearing aid mode, simply tap the toggle switch so that it turns from green (indicating it's turned on) to white (indicating it's turned off). Once you have done this, hearing aid mode will be disabled on your iPhone. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Keep in mind that disabling hearing aid mode may affect audio quality when using hearing aids. If you experience any issues with audio after disabling this feature, consider re-enabling it. * Some third-party apps or accessories may require hearing aid mode to function properly. Double check whether this is the case before turning off the feature. * To ensure optimal performance, always keep your iPhone up to date with the latest software updates. These updates often include bug fixes and improvements to existing features, including those related to accessibility. By following these clear and concise steps, you can successfully disable hearing aid mode on your iPhone. Remember to only make changes to your device's settings if you are comfortable doing so, and seek assistance if needed. What does she have that's clean, I wonder. Title: How to Help a Baby Sit Up Introduction: Helping a baby sit up is an important milestone in their development. It not only strengthens their core muscles but also aids in their overall physical and mental growth. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to help your baby sit up, along with useful tips and guidelines to ensure safety and effectiveness. Step 1: Understand the Right Age Typically, babies can start sitting up with support around 4-6 months of age. However, every child is unique and may reach this milestone at different times. Before attempting to help your baby sit up, make sure they have good head control and are able to hold their heads steady without much assistance. Key Tip: Never rush or force your baby into sitting up before they're ready. Always consult with your pediatrician if you have any concerns about your baby's development. Step 2: Create a Safe Environment Choose a clean, open space free from hard edges or sharp objects where your baby can practice sitting up. Place soft mats or blankets underneath them for added comfort and protection. Guideline: Always supervise your baby during these exercises to prevent accidents and injuries. Step 3: Use Supportive Pillows or Boppy Place supportive pillows or a boppy pillow around your baby to give them extra stability while learning to sit up. Make sure the padding is firm enough to keep them upright yet comfortable enough so as not to restrict breathing or movement. Key Tip: Avoid using large, bulky pillows that could potentially tip over and cause injury. Step 4: Encourage Tummy Time Tummy time helps build strength in your baby's neck, back, and shoulder muscles - all essential components for sitting up independently. Start by placing your baby on their tummy for short periods throughout the day, gradually increasing the duration as they grow stronger. Step 5: Gradual Progression Begin by supporting your baby in a seated position using one hand under their chest and another hand behind their lower back. Slowly reduce the amount of support you provide until they can maintain balance on their own. Be patient; this process might take several weeks or even months depending on your baby's individual progress. Step 6: Introduce Toys and Interactive Play Use colorful toys or books to engage your baby during sitting sessions. Place the items slightly out of reach to encourage them to lean forward and stretch, further developing their core muscles and coordination skills. Step 7: Practice Makes Perfect Consistency is crucial when helping your baby learn to sit up. Regular practice will not only reinforce muscle memory but also foster confidence in your little one as they master this new skill. Key Tips: * Alternate between supported and unsupported sitting sessions to challenge your baby and promote continued improvement. * Remember to always prioritize safety and never leave your baby unattended during these activities. Conclusion: With patience, persistence, and careful guidance, you can successfully help your baby sit up and enjoy witnessing their incredible journey towards achieving this significant milestone. As always, consult with your pediatrician if you have any questions or concerns regarding your baby's development. let's see what's under the tepee. Here why don't can you put that one on yourself? It seemed to be a fly. He darted over to look more closely. "What is this thing?" He asked. As he investigated, he heard a little voice. It was the fly speaking. She said, "My name is Daisy. I am here to reveal something to you." Timmy was surprised, but he asked Daisy to tell him what the secret was. She replied, "The secret is that everyone needs to be kind and helpful to each other. We can all work together to make the world a better place." Timmy was so excited about Daisy's secret and he couldn't wait to tell everyone. He wanted to make sure that everyone he knew knew the secret too, so that the world could become a better place. Boosting your chances of getting a review on FanFiction.net can significantly enhance your writing experience and help you grow as an author. Reviews provide valuable feedback, encouragement, and constructive criticism that can guide you in improving your stories and characters. Here's a comprehensive, step-by-step guide on how to increase your odds of receiving reviews: ### Step 1: Write High-Quality Content * Explanation: Writing engaging, well-edited, and unique fanfictions is essential to attracting potential reviewers. Good content showcases your dedication, creativity, and passion for storytelling. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Focus on proper grammar, spelling, and punctuation. + Create compelling narratives with intriguing plotlines and character development. + Be original; avoid copying or heavily borrowing from existing works. ### Step 2: Choose Popular Fandoms * Explanation: By selecting popular fandoms, you tap into larger audiences who are already invested in those universes, increasing the likelihood they will engage with your work through reviews. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Select widely recognized fandoms such as Harry Potter, Marvel Cinematic Universe, or Star Wars. + Research the popularity of various fandoms before committing to one. + Keep up with current trends within chosen fandom(s) to stay relevant. ### Step 3: Post Regularly * Explanation: Consistent posting schedules encourage reader engagement, making them more likely to leave reviews due to their emotional investment in your ongoing narrative. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Establish a reasonable posting schedule based on your availability (e.g., weekly, biweekly). Once upon a time there was a smart bear named Chris. He loved exploring the forest and looking for new things. One day, as he was walking through the forest, he observed some nice fruit on a tree. He wanted to try it, so he climbed onto the tree and picked the fruit with his paws. When he tasted the fruit, it was sweeter than anything he had ever tasted before! He was so happy that he started to jump up and down in joy. While he enjoyed the fruit, a rabbit came hopping by. The rabbit saw the bear and asked him, "What are you doing?" Chris smiled and said, "I'm eating the yummy fruit I found on this tree and it's so delicious!" The rabbit's eyes widened and he said, "Wow, I want some too!" Chris laughed and said, "Come on up! I'll share it with you." So the bear and the rabbit enjoyed the tasty fruit together, and they became the best of friends. From then on the two of them were always seen exploring the forest and discovering new things. Title: How to Delete a Spotify Playlist on Your iPhone or iPad Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting a playlist from your Spotify account using an iPhone or iPad. Whether you've created a playlist by mistake, want to declutter your library, or simply don't enjoy the music anymore, this step-by-step guide has got you covered. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Make sure you have the latest version of the Spotify app installed on your device. * Deleting a playlist is permanent; there is no way to recover deleted playlists. * If you only wish to remove songs from a playlist without deleting the entire list, refer to our companion tutorial on 'How to Remove Songs from a Spotify Playlist.' Step 1: Open the Spotify App On your iPhone or iPad home screen, locate and tap the Spotify icon to open the application. The Spotify logo features a black circle with three curved green lines inside. Step 2: Log into Your Account (if necessary) If you aren't already logged in, enter your email address or username along with your password to access your Spotify account. This step ensures that you can manage your personal playlists. Step 3: Navigate to Your Library Tap the 'Your Library' tab located at the bottom right corner of the screen. Here, you'll find all of your saved playlists, artists, albums, and podcasts. Step 4: Select the Playlist You Want to Delete Scroll down until you find the playlist you wish to delete. Tapping on the playlist cover art will take you to the track listing within that specific playlist. To proceed, ensure that you are viewing the correct playlist. Step 5: Access Playlist Settings Locate and tap the ellipsis ('...') button found in the upper right corner of the screen. A dropdown menu should appear containing various options related to the selected playlist. Step 6: Choose 'Delete Playlist' Option From the dropdown menu, select the option labeled 'Delete Playlist'. Upon tapping, a confirmation message will pop up asking if you truly want to delete the playlist. Keep in mind that this action cannot be undone! Step 7: Confirm Deletion Review the name of the playlist displayed in the confirmation prompt. If you are certain about removing this playlist permanently, tap 'DELETE'. Afterward, you will be redirected back to your main library view where the deleted playlist will no longer appear. Conclusion: Congratulations! By following these steps, you successfully removed a playlist from your Spotify account via your iPhone or iPad. Regularly maintaining your playlists enhances your overall user experience, making it easier to discover new tracks and revisit old favorites. XXX glad you're here I'm goin' to get some licorice. Then the pupils read the sentence in turn, supplying the translation of the words as they are rapidly pointed out. A few moments' work of this kind suffices with average pupils to enable them to memorize the words so that they can reproduce them verbally or in writing, when the book is shut or after they have been rubbed off the board. The next sentence is treated in the same way, the pupils translating the words previously given and the instructor giving the meaning _of the new words only_. After three unsuccessful attempts, Hugo was finally elected to the Acad mie fran aise in 1841, confirming his position in the world of French arts and letters. A group of French scholars, particularly Etienne de Jouy, were fighting against the "romantic evolution" and had managed to delay Victor Hugo's election. After that he became increasingly involved in French politics. He was raised to the peerage by King Louis-Philippe in 1841 and entered the Higher Chamber as a "pair de France", where he spoke against the death penalty and social injustice, and in favour of freedom of the press and self-government for Poland. However, he was also becoming more supportive of the Republican form of government and, following the 1848 Revolution and the formation of the Second Republic, was elected to the Constitutional Assembly and the Legislative Assembly. Emphasizes last part of word, sound of y Can I have one of your carrots? But Aaron has a lot [lots] of sisters and brothers. Title: Understanding Ship Speeds and Direction Hello young explorers! Today, we will learn about speed and direction using ships as our example. Let's imagine two magical ships, A and B, moving on a grid map where North-South and East-West axes meet at a point called the origin (where both ships start). 1. Speed and movement: Ship A moves South at 24 kilometers per hour (km/h), while ship B starts 48 km directly South of A and sails East at 18 km/h. To understand their progress, let us first grasp their individual movements: * Picture a vertical line representing the journey of Ship A; it covers 24 km every hour heading South. * Now imagine a horizontal line symbolizing Ship B's path, traveling 18 km each hour towards the East. (Note: They won't physically collide because Ship B goes East, and A travels South.) 1. Rate of Approach: Now comes the fun part - finding their combined speed and direction! After one hour, Ship A would have moved 24 km downwards, while B advances 18 km eastward. We need to know how quickly they grow closer together or separate. In fact, they APPROACH each other since initially, they move toward decreasing the distance between them before eventually going further apart. To calculate the closing rate, we must consider both ships' speeds along the hypotenuse of a right triangle formed by joining their positions after an hour. Using Pythagorean theorem (a + b = c ), we get: (24 km) + (18 km) = c . So, c = sqrt[(24 km) + (18 km) ] = ~31.07 km. But remember, this isn't the actual distance covered; rather, it indicates the "combined speed." Since they started 48 km apart, the remaining gap reduces by around 31.07 km each hour. After several hours, however, the remaining distance becomes negative, meaning they begin separating instead of growing closer. This happens roughly within four hours of starting the voyage. 1. Ceasing to approach: The second question asks when they stop getting nearer. As mentioned earlier, this occurs approximately four hours into their journeys. The exact moment is when the residual distance equals zero. But why does this matter? Well, once the leftover space turns positive again, it signifies no longer being influenced by each other's initial proximity. It also highlights the importance of understanding relative motion and distances in real life. In summary, through our magical adventure with Ships A & B, we learned key aspects of speed, direction, and relative motion. By breaking complex scenarios into smaller parts, identifying relationships, and applying basic mathematical principles like the Pythagorean theorem, even challenging questions become accessible and enjoyable! New mountains are built when rocks are pushed upwards by the movement of the giant rocky plates that make up the Earth's crust. The rocks are pushed upwards in two ways: FOLD mountains are formed when layers of rock become buckled, and BLOCK mountains are formed when giant lumps of rock rise or fall. Volcanic eruptions also create mountains. Many mountain ranges have been built up and eroded away since the Earth was formed. The Andes is the longest mountain range on land. It was formed along the western margin of South America, where two tectonic plates (rocky plates that make up the Earth's crust) collided. The mountains are still rising by about 10 cm (4 in) every century. The world's major mountain ranges, such as the Andes, the Himalayas, and the Alps, are situated along the boundaries where tectonic plates collide. These ranges formed in the last few hundred million years, so are they quite young. The map also shows thin lines of volcanoes that erupt from the ocean floor, forming chains of mountainous islands. The Himalayas is a range of fold mountains formed by the collision between India and the rest of Asia. When the two tectonic plates collided, the southern edge of Asia buckled. The Indian plate continues to slide under Asia and, to date, has uplifted Tibet to a height of over 5 km (3 miles). When layers of rock are pushed inwards from both ends, they crumple up into waves called folds. Rocks are too hard to be squashed into a smaller space. Instead they fold upwards and downwards. The immense forces that cause folding can crunch solid rocks into folds just a few metres across. The rocks that buckle to form fold mountains are made up of layers of sedimentary rocks and igneous rocks. When the layers are folded, the rocks on the outside of a fold are stretched and the rocks on the inside of a fold are squashed. The folding also makes the layers of rock slide over each other. Block mountains are mountains formed when layers of rock crack into giant blocks. Cracks in layers of rock are called faults. They form when the Earth's crust is stretched, squashed, or twisted. The blocks are free to slip up, down, or sideways, or to tip over. These movements are very slow, but over millions of years they form mountains thousands of metres high. If there is little light shining on a plant, the light-dependent reactions will not work efficiently. This means that photolysis (breakdown of water by light) will not happen quickly, and therefore little NADPH and ATP will be made. This shortage of NADPH and ATP will lead to the light-independent reactions not working as NADPH and ATP are needed for the light-independent reactions to work. Problem Solving Strategies This chapter is aimed at solving mathematical problems. In solving these problems, you have to know what you are trying to solve and find the correct process to go about it. Therefore, you also need to know the basic procedures and techniques in mathematics. Problem solving could be summarized using these steps: - Read and understand the problem carefully. - Identify what the problem asked for. - Take note of the data the problem is presenting to you. - Determine the possible process you are going to do and use the applicable data in the problem. - Check if you really answered what the problem asked. Mr. X has to be at work by 8:00 AM and it would take him 10 minutes to get dressed, 15 minutes to eat, and 40 minutes to ride to his work. What time should he get up to get to his work on time? What is the problem asking? You should know what time Mr. X would get up in order for him to get to work on time. What are the data presented in the problem? 10 minutes - to get dressed 15 minutes - to eat 40 minutes - travel time to his work What process should we use in order to solve the problem? You need to know the total number of minutes Mr. X takes to prepare before he goes to his work and subtract it from his target time to be able to know what time he should get up. 10 + 15 + 40 = 65 minutes Mr. X needs 65 minutes in order for him to prepare and be ready for work. 65 minutes is also equivalent to 1 hour and 5 minutes. Now that we know how long he prepares himself, we can now deduct it from the target time which is 8:00 AM. 8:00 AM - 65 minutes = 6:55 AM Therefore, Mr. X should be awake by 6:55 AM so that he can be at work by 8:00 AM. Sammy sells lemonade during summer. On the first week, he sold 32 glasses, on the second he sold 30 glasses and on the last week he sold 35 glasses. How many glasses did he sell for 3 weeks? The problem is asking the total number of glasses of lemonade Sammy sold. First week - 32 glasses Second week - 30 glasses Third week - 35 glasses You just add up all of the sold glasses of lemonade 32 + 30 + 35 = 97 Therefore, Sammy sold 97 glasses of lemonade in three weeks. - Mr. Rodriguez and his family went on a picnic in the countryside. They had to buy food first so they went to the nearest grocery along the way which is 5 km from their house. Then they took a 53 km trip to the countryside. However, they got lost along the way and passed the picnic grounds. They then had to drive back 8km. How far was the countryside from Mr. Rodriguez's house? The problem wants you to solve how many kilometres is the countryside from the house of Mr. Rodriguez. From their house to the grocery - 5 km From the grocery to the countryside - 53 km The distance they got lost - 8 km We add up the distance from their house to the grocery and from the grocery to the countryside. 5 km + 53 km = 58 km However, the problem said that they drove back 8 km because they got lost. So, we subtract the distance they drove back. 58 km - 8 km = 50 km Therefore, the distance from the house of Mr. Rodriguez to the countryside is 50 km. The sign in the grocery says "one free for every 2 purchase of each item". How many would you purchase if you wanted to get 6 free items? The problem asks how many items you should buy to get 6 free items. 2 items = 1 free You want to get 6 free items. So, 1 free x 6 pieces = 2 items x 6 6 free items = 12 items Therefore, in order to get 6 free items, you have to purchase 12 items. Find Assignments Which Have Not Yet Been Graded in Biggyan Cloud Elearning Biggyan Cloud Elearning is an online learning management system that allows teachers and students to access course materials, submit assignments, participate in discussions, and track progress. One common task for instructors using this platform is finding assignments which have not yet been graded. This tutorial will provide you with a step-by-step guide on how to accomplish this efficiently within Biggyan Cloud Elearning. **Time required:** Approximately 5 minutes **Materials needed:** A computer or mobile device with internet connection and valid login credentials for your Biggyan Cloud Elearning account. Step 1: Log into Your Account ------------------------------ Visit <https://www.biggyanclecloud.com/> and click on 'Sign In' at the top right corner. Enter your registered email address and password then hit 'Login'. Once logged in, you should be directed to the dashboard where all courses are listed. *Key Tip*: Make sure you remember your login details as they're crucial for accessing any information related to your courses and grades. If forgotten, utilize the 'Forgot Password?' link under the sign-in button. Step 2: Navigate to Course Homepage ----------------------------------- From the dashboard, locate the specific course for which you want to check ungraded assignments. Click on its title or icon to open the course homepage. Step 3: Access Gradebook ------------------------ On the left side menu of the course page, look for 'Gradebook' (usually located near the bottom). Click on it to view the gradebook for this particular course. *Guideline*: The location of certain features may vary slightly depending upon the theme used by your institution; however, most options remain consistent across different themes. Step 4: Filter Ungraded Items ----------------------------- After entering the gradebook, find the filter function usually situated above the list of assignments and students. Look for the option named 'View' or similar, then select 'Ungraded'. By doing so, only submissions awaiting grading will appear on screen. *Tip*: Some platforms might require multiple clicks to reach the final stage due to nested filters. Always ensure you've selected both the correct assignment category and student status before proceeding. Step 5: Review & Grade Submissions ---------------------------------- Now you can see all the pending assignments. To begin grading, simply click on the name of the submission. You'll typically see two views - either a direct preview of the submitted work or a separate window providing tools to input scores and feedback. *Note*: Depending on settings configured by your instructor, some assignments may need manual switching between student view and grading interface. Congratulations! You have successfully found assignments which have not yet been graded in Biggyan Cloud Elearning! Remember these steps whenever you need to manage your grading tasks effectively. Title: How to Enable Dark Theme on Gboard (Google Keyboard) Gboard, also known as Google Keyboard, is a popular keyboard app developed by Google. It offers various features including gesture typing, voice typing, emoji search, and GIFs. One such feature that has gained popularity recently is the dark theme. The dark theme can help reduce eye strain while using your device in low-light conditions or at night. This tutorial will guide you through enabling the dark theme on Gboard. **Step 1: Open the Settings App** The first step is to open the settings app on your Android device. The settings app is usually represented by a gear icon and can be found in the app drawer or on the home screen. Key Tips & Guidelines: - If you're having trouble finding the settings app, try using the search function on your device. - Make sure you have the latest version of the settings app installed. **Step 2: Navigate to System Settings** Once you've opened the settings app, look for a section labeled 'System,' 'Device,' or something similar. Tap on this section to enter the system settings menu. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Depending on your device model and manufacturer, the layout and labels may vary slightly. - Be cautious when changing settings within this section; some options might affect overall system performance or functionality. **Step 3: Select Display Options** Within the system settings menu, find an option called 'Display,' 'Screen,' or something similar and tap to open it. Here, you should see several display-related settings. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Again, depending on your device, these options might differ slightly from one another. However, they generally serve the same purpose across devices. **Step 4: Locate Dark Mode Setting** Scroll down until you find the 'Dark mode' setting. This option allows you to enable a system-wide dark theme which affects all compatible apps, including Gboard. Toggle the switch next to 'Dark mode' to activate it. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Some devices may require you to manually enable dark themes for individual applications even after enabling system-wide dark mode. - Keep in mind that not all apps support dark mode yet, so certain elements within those apps could appear lighter than others. **Step 5: Open Gboard** After successfully activating dark mode, launch any application where text input is required. For instance, send a message via WhatsApp or compose an email using Gmail. When the virtual keyboard appears, ensure it is indeed Gboard by checking its logo at the top corner. Key Tips & Guidelines: - You can double check if you are using Gboard by going into your phone's language and input settings. - Don't confuse other third-party keyboards with Gboard. They might not offer the same functionalities. **Step 6: Customize Gboard Appearance** Tap on the Gboard logo located at the top left corner of the keyboard. A new menu should slide up from below. Look for 'Theme' or 'Design' and select it. Now choose the preferred dark theme among available options. Key Tips & Guidelines: - There could be multiple dark themes available offering different shades or designs. Choose whichever suits your preference best. - After selecting a new theme, make sure to hit 'Apply' or save changes before exiting the settings page. By following these steps carefully, you would have enabled the dark theme for Gboard effectively reducing eye strain during late-night chatting sessions! Enjoy exploring more customization possibilities offered by Gboard. But they stick good to a magnet. I think a lot of XXX daycare there's... Continents on MarsWhen you first look at a global picture of Mars, the two things which stand out the most are the two regions which are colored red and yellow in the picture to the left. They seem elevated above the rest of the crust. The largest red-and-yellow region covers the lower half of the planet. This region is called the Highlands. The rest of the surface is called Lowlands. The highlands seem like a continent on Earth. The other feature is a smaller, island-shaped feature, known as the Tharsis Ridge. It is also as large as a small continent on Earth, or perhaps a large, volcanic island. These two features may be thought of as the "continents of Mars". Title: How to Check What Version of Internet Explorer You Are Using Introduction: Internet Explorer (IE) is one of the most widely used web browsers developed by Microsoft. It has been an integral part of the Windows operating system since its inception. With various versions released over time, understanding which version you are using can be beneficial for troubleshooting issues, ensuring compatibility with websites, or even deciding whether an upgrade is necessary. This comprehensive guide will walk you through checking your current IE version in detail, providing helpful insights along the way. Step 1: Launch Internet Explorer To begin, locate and open the Internet Explorer application on your computer. Typically, this icon appears as a blue 'e' on a white background either pinned to your taskbar, found within your Start menu, or accessible via the 'All Programs' list under 'Windows Accessories.' Once opened, proceed to Step 2. Key Tip: If you cannot find Internet Explorer on your system, consider searching for it using the search bar located near the Start button or asking a knowledgeable friend or family member for assistance. Step 2: Access Help & Support Once Internet Explorer is running, look towards the top right corner where several icons are displayed. Identify the gear-shaped icon labeled 'Settings,' then click on it. From the dropdown menu that appears, select 'Help' followed by 'About Internet Explorer.' By doing so, you initiate the process of retrieving information about your installed IE version. Guideline: Remember that some older versions may display slightly different menus; however, the overall steps should remain consistent. Step 3: Interpret Displayed Information Upon selecting 'About Internet Explorer', a new window will appear showcasing details regarding your browser's configuration, including the version number. The format usually follows: "MajorVersion.MinorVersion BuildNumber." For instance, if your screen displays "Internet Explorer 11.0.9600," it means you are currently utilizing Internet Explorer version 11. Key Terminology: MajorVersion refers to significant updates introducing substantial changes while MinorVersion signifies incremental improvements made during the lifecycle of the major release. BuildNumber indicates specific builds rolled out between minor revisions. Step 4: Cross Verify Results Online (Optional) For added confirmation, cross-verify your obtained results online using reputable sources such as Microsoft's official website. Navigate to their support page listing all available Internet Explorer versions and compare yours against those listed. This verification ensures accuracy and provides reassurance. Conclusion: By following these simple yet informative steps, you now understand how to check what version of Internet Explorer you have. Regularly performing this action allows you to stay informed about potential security patches, bug fixes, and functional enhancements associated with newer releases. Moreover, sharing this knowledge with others could help them maintain optimal performance and security when using their web browsers too! Then he climbed down the beanstalk as fast as he could go. You have been studying several ways that Earth's surface is built up. In this activity, you and a family member can see how the Himalaya Mountains were formed. You will model plates of Earth's crust crashing together! - modeling clay - plastic wrap, waxed paper, or construction paper Ask an adult to help you make two slabs of modeling clay, each about an inch thick. Cut out a piece of plastic wrap, waxed paper, or construction paper the same size as each square of clay. Place them under each piece of clay. This will help the clay slide along your work surface. Slide the pieces of clay into each other, and continue pressing together after they touch. Repeat this process by sliding the pieces at different speeds and by pressing with different amounts of force. Observe the results. What happened after the pieces of clay came into contact and were pushed together? Did one piece slide under the other? Did they both go up where they came into contact? Did "mountains" form at the point where they came into contact or away from there? Did the speed or force change the results. oh, thank you for putting the trash away. Title: How to Avoid Getting Your Image Deleted from wikiHow Introduction: wikiHow is a collaborative platform that relies on user-generated content, including images. However, there are certain rules and guidelines that you need to follow when uploading an image to ensure that it remains on the site. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to avoid getting your image deleted from wikiHow. Step 1: Understand Copyright Laws Before uploading any image, make sure you understand copyright laws. You cannot simply take an image from anywhere on the internet and upload it to wikiHow. The image must be your own original work or you must have permission from the copyright holder to use it. If you're using someone else's work, even if it's under a Creative Commons license, always give proper attribution. Failure to comply with copyright laws can result in immediate deletion of your image. Key Tip: Familiarize yourself with fair use doctrine which allows limited use of copyrighted material without obtaining permission from the rights holders. However, this is a complex area of law and should be approached with caution. Step 2: Check wikiHow's Image Policy wikiHow has specific policies regarding what types of images are acceptable. These include but are not limited to: * Relevance: Images should directly relate to the topic of the article. * Quality: High resolution images preferred; no blurry or low-quality photos. * Size: Shouldn't exceed 2MB. * Format: Acceptable formats are .jpg, .jpeg, .png, and .gif. * No watermarks or promotional text. Review these guidelines carefully before submitting your image. Step 3: Use Appropriate Content Ensure that your image does not contain any offensive, violent, sexual, or discriminatory content. Also, avoid graphic depictions of injuries, medical procedures, etc., as they may be considered disturbing by some users. Remember, wikiHow aims to create helpful, family-friendly content. Step 4: Properly Categorize Your Image When uploading your image, choose appropriate categories so that it can be properly indexed and found by other users. Misleading categorization could lead to removal of your image. Step 5: Provide Accurate Description Along with your image, provide a clear and concise description detailing what the image shows. This helps visually impaired users understand the context better through screen reader tools. It also facilitates search functionality within wikiHow. Step 6: Respond Promptly to Feedback After uploading an image, keep an eye out for feedback from the community. They might point out issues like incorrect licensing, poor quality, or irrelevant content. Address such concerns promptly to prevent your image from being deleted. Step 7: Regularly Review Your Uploads Periodically review your uploaded images to ensure they still meet all current standards and policies. Policies do change over time, and an image that was once compliant might now violate updated rules. Conclusion: By following these steps, you significantly increase the chances of keeping your images live on wikiHow. Always remember that user collaboration drives this platform, and respecting others' works while contributing yours fairly fosters a healthy community. I want them to flash so they can tell the... Activity type: Brainstorm & Discussion - BEFORE students arrive, WRITE "RESPECT" on the board. - ASK: What does RESPECT mean? Brainstorm definitions/synonyms. - ASK: Why is treating others with respect important in groups? - ASK: What does it looks like when you treat someone with respect? Give an example that you've experienced in the past day or so. Examples (if kids are stuck): Say "please" and "thank you." Return things that people lend to you. Listen when someone else is talking. - ASK: What does it look like when someone disrespects you? Give an example from your own (recent) experience. Examples (if kids are stuck): People make fun of the way I look. Don't listen to what I have to say. Push me around or threaten me. - ASK: What is self-respect? What's an example of someone acting like they have self-respect? What behavior might indicate low self- respect? How does self-respect connect with treating others respectfully? - EMPHASIZE: Communities work best when we treat each other with respect. Since the Belfast Agreement (sometimes called the Good Friday Agreement) of Friday, 10 April 1998, there has been mainly peace between the two communities in Northern Ireland, the Protestants and Catholics. This agreement was agreed by most of the people in Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland, as well as the Irish and British governments. It allows for the self-government of Northern Ireland and greater North-South co-operation and co-operation between Britain and the Republic of Ireland. Additionally, it makes clear the right of the people of Northern Ireland to decide their constitutional future and select whether they are British citizens, Irish citizens or both. His first album with Wells, "Together", became Marvin's first charted album, and yielded two popular hits, while his single with Weston, the Sylvia Moy-penned "It Takes Two", hit the top twenty on both sides of the Atlantic. [Fei is looking at the other page] A river is a stream of water that flows through a channel in the surface of the ground. The passage where the river flows is called the river bed and the earth on each side is called a river bank. A river begins on high ground or in hills or mountains and flows down from the high ground to the lower ground, because of gravity. A river begins as a small stream, and gets bigger the farther it flows. Look we haven't played with any of the stuffed animals yet. Look at the toothpick pattern and complete Problems B1-B8. Groups: Work in pairs. There are two common ways of thinking about the toothpick pattern, and one of the reasons it's useful to work in pairs or small groups is to observe this variety. Some people see the pattern as groups of 4 toothpicks, with 2 toothpicks added on to the right-most triangle, and they come up with the following rule: Take the number of triangles, multiply by 4, and add 2. Others see the pattern as groups of 6 toothpicks, with 2 toothpicks removed from all of the triangles except the right-most triangle. The rule they come up with is: Take the number of triangles, multiply by 6, and then subtract 2 times 1 less than the number of triangles. When using a variable, the first rule turns out to be 4n + 2; the second becomes 6n - 2(n-1). The second rule may be harder to represent "subtract 2 times 1 less than the number of triangles" can be tricky to express. Consider going through a derivation of this rule, starting with: If the number of triangles is 5, what s 1 less? So we subtract 2 times that. If the number of triangles is 10, what s 1 less? Then subtract 2 times that number. Finally, if the number of triangles is n, what s 1 less? It is n - 1. The second difficulty may be seeing the need for the parentheses when multiplying this quantity by 2. Explore the expressions 2(n - 1) and 2n - 1, testing some particular values for n to decide which accurately describes "2 times 1 less than n." Those who come up with a symbolic rule right away, without even looking at the drawing, should think about where the "4-ness" or the "6-ness" comes from in the picture. Make sure to make the link between what's going on in the equation and what's happening in the picture. Groups: No matter how people come up with them, explore the two different rules as a full group. Do they continue the table in the same way? Are the rules equivalent, even though they look different? Title: How to Draw Hedwig from Harry Potter ## Step 1: Gather Your Materials Before you begin drawing Hedwig, make sure you have the necessary materials. You will need: * A piece of paper * A pencil for sketching * An eraser * Colored pencils or markers for adding color (optional) Having these tools ready will ensure that your drawing process is smooth and enjoyable. ## Step 2: Lightly Sketch an Oval Shape Start by lightly sketching an oval shape on your paper using your pencil. This will serve as the basic guideline for Hedwig's head and body. Make the oval slightly elongated to accommodate her neck and body. Keep in mind that this is just a guide; you can adjust its size and proportions according to your preference. ## Step 3: Add Guidelines for Facial Features Divide the oval into four equal sections horizontally and vertically with light lines. These divisions will help you place Hedwig's eyes, beak, and other facial features accurately within the head shape. The horizontal line at the center should indicate where her eyes are positioned, while the vertical line serves as a reference point for symmetry. ## Step 4: Outline Hedwig's Body Now, draw two curved lines extending downwards from the bottom part of the oval. Connect them gently with another curve forming a rounded triangle-like figure representing Hedwig's chest area. Afterward, add two small circles below this section to represent her legs. Remember to keep everything loose and flexible since we're still in the sketching phase. ## Step 5: Draw Hedwig's Eyes Look closely at Hedwig's appearance - she has large, round, expressive eyes. Using the guidelines drawn earlier, position two identical circles above the central horizontal line. Each circle should touch the upper division line but leave enough space between them for eyebrows. ## Step 6: Create Realistic Eyebrows Birds like Hedwig don't have human-like eyebrows, so instead, create subtle arches above her eyes. To do this, simply draw short, thin curves arching upwards near the inner corners of her eyes, then gradually becoming thinner towards the outer edges. ## Step 7: Form Hedwig's Beak Just below the lower division line, draw an elongated letter 'V'. It should lean slightly forward to resemble a parrot's beak. Notice that the left side of the 'V' starts narrower than the right side, creating a more natural look. ## Step 8: Design Feathers Around Her Face Feathers are essential when drawing birds. Start adding feathery details around Hedwig's face by drawing several overlapping U-shaped curves along the sides of her head. They should start wide near the base of her ears and taper off close to her beak. Also, include some shorter strokes inside these shapes to imply individual feathers. ## Step 9: Detail Her Wing You should be outside playin' instead of playin' Nintendo. A hoe is a tool in farming and gardening. It has a blade, usually metal, attached to a long handle, usually wood. There are many types of blade and several uses, probably the most common of which is weeding. Other uses include shaping the soil and harvesting root crops such as potatoes. Along with the spade and fork, the hoe is considered a basic, essential hand farming implement. Population ecology measures the size of a population: all the living things from one species that live in an place. A population gets bigger because of birth and movement into a place, and it gets smaller because of death and movement out of a place. Growth rate is the change in population size divided by the current population size. When a population is small, growth rate does not change, so the population shows exponential growth. Rate of exponential growth depends on how a living thing reproduces. If it has only a few offspring (children) which grow slowly, like a human, the rate will be low. If it has a lot of offspring which grow quickly, like a fruit fly, the rate will be high. Any environment only has enough resources, such as food, water, or space, for a certain size of population. This size is called the carrying capacity. When population size is near the carrying capacity, growth rate will become less. The graph of population growth will be an S-shape, called logistic growth. Disable Onboard Sound: A Comprehensive Guide ============================================= Introduction ------------ This guide will walk you through the process of disabling your computer's onboard sound. This can be useful if you have an external audio device that you prefer to use or if you are experiencing issues with your onboard sound. By following these steps, you can ensure that your onboard sound is turned off and not interfering with any other audio devices. ### Key Tips and Guidelines * Before making any changes to your system settings, make sure to create a restore point. This will allow you to revert back to the previous state of your system if something goes wrong. * Make sure to carefully read and understand each step before proceeding. If you are unsure about anything, do some research or seek help from a professional. * Keep in mind that this process may vary depending on your specific hardware and software configurations. The steps outlined in this guide should work for most systems, but there may be some variations. Step 1: Access Device Manager ----------------------------- The first step in disabling your onboard sound is to access the Device Manager. This can be done by right-clicking on the Start button (or pressing Windows + X) and selecting Device Manager from the menu. Alternatively, you can press the Windows key + R to open the Run dialog box, type `devmgmt.msc` and hit Enter. Device Manager provides a central location where you can view all of the devices connected to your computer and manage their drivers and settings. It is important to note that making changes to your devices in Device Manager can affect the overall functionality of your system, so be careful when making any adjustments. Step 2: Locate the Audio Devices ------------------------------ Once you have opened Device Manager, look for the category labeled "Sound, video and game controllers." Expand this category by clicking on the arrow next to it. You should see a list of audio devices, including your onboard sound. ![Device Manager](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Disable-Onboard-Sound-Step-2-Version-3.jpg/aid4567809-v4- Take one of these things [silver stick] maybe you'll pick one of the guys [silver men] up. Title: How to Convince Your Parents that ADHD Medication is Affecting Your Concentration Introduction: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder affecting both children and adults. It can impact an individual's ability to focus, control impulses, and manage emotions. When diagnosed with ADHD, medication might be recommended as part of the treatment plan. However, if you believe your current medication isn't working or causing side effects like reduced concentration, this guide will help you communicate effectively with your parents about your concerns. Remember, open communication is essential when discussing any changes to your medical treatment. Step 1: Understand Your Symptoms and Reactions Before talking to your parents, take time to reflect on your experiences with your ADHD medication. Identify specific instances where you felt the medication negatively affected your concentration. Be prepared to share examples from school, work, or social situations. This self-awareness will demonstrate maturity and commitment to managing your condition. Key Tips: * Keep a journal of your symptoms and reactions. * Note down dates, times, dosages, activities, and feelings during these episodes. Step 2: Research Alternative Treatments Familiarize yourself with alternative treatments for ADHD, such as behavioral therapy, dietary modifications, exercise routines, and other medications. Showing your parents that you have taken initiative to learn about alternatives indicates responsibility and willingness to explore different options. Guidelines: * Use reputable sources like Mayo Clinic, CDC, or NIH websites for research. * Make sure to understand the potential benefits and risks associated with each alternative. Step 3: Plan a Calm and Respectful Conversation Choose a suitable time and place for the conversation. Request their full attention, ensuring there are no distractions. Speak calmly and respectfully, expressing your thoughts clearly and honestly without blaming anyone. Key Points: * Avoid accusatory language; instead, use "I feel" statements. * Listen carefully to their responses and validate their feelings. Step 4: Present Facts and Observations Share your observations regarding the impact of your current medication on your concentration. Use concrete examples from your daily life to illustrate your points. Refer back to your journal if needed. Additional Guidelines: * Stick to facts and avoid exaggerating or minimizing your experiences. * Remain objective and focused on your main concern - the effect of the medication on your concentration. Step 5: Propose Solutions and Next Steps Suggest possible solutions based on your research into alternative treatments. Explain why you think they could be more effective than your current medication. Offer to schedule an appointment with your healthcare provider together to discuss changing your treatment plan. Important Considerations: * Be flexible and open to compromise. * Recognize that change may take time and patience. Conclusion: Communicating openly with your parents about your concerns regarding your ADHD medication is crucial. By following these steps, you can foster understanding, collaboration, and informed decision-making around your treatment. Always remember that finding the right balance in managing ADHD requires teamwork, persistence, and adaptability. I was named Julius, after this uncle, who was my godfather, and who was much older than was my father, and who, although he had never seen me, yet took great interest in me, and mentioned me in all his letters. It was just before my tenth birthday that my father received a letter from my uncle, which caused a great change in my life, and led to those adventures which I relate in this tale. In this letter my uncle wrote, that from his experience of India he was certain that I could not be properly educated in that country; that at my age the climate was very trying; and that consequently he wished my father to send me home, in order that I might be placed at a good school in England, and eventually sent either to Addiscombe or Haileybury, according as I chose the military or civil service of India. The expenses of my education, my uncle stated, would be undertaken by him, so that money need not interfere with the question. Young as I was I saw the advantages of this proposition, and being by nature ambitious and fond of adventure, I was pleased at the prospect of seeing England. Applying for a hardship loan can be a crucial step in overcoming financial difficulties during challenging times. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process with clear instructions, helpful tips, and important considerations at each stage. By following these steps diligently, you'll increase your chances of successfully obtaining the funds needed to alleviate your financial strain. ### Step 1: Assess Your Financial Situation Before applying for a hardship loan, take an honest look at your current finances. Determine the root cause of your financial distress by examining income sources, expenses, debts, savings, and credit score. Understanding your situation allows you to identify whether a hardship loan is truly necessary and if so, which type best suits your needs. Key tip: Be thorough when assessing your finances; this self-evaluation enables informed decision making throughout the application process. ### Step 2: Explore Different Types of Hardship Loans There are various types of hardship loans available, including personal loans from banks or credit unions, payday alternative loans (PALs), peer-to-peer lending, home equity loans, and 401(k) withdrawals. Research each option thoroughly to understand their terms, interest rates, repayment schedules, eligibility requirements, and potential impact on credit scores. Guideline: Choose the loan that aligns closest with your unique circumstances and offers favorable conditions. ### Step 3: Gather Required Documents Prepare essential documents such as proof of identity (driver's license or passport), recent tax returns, bank statements, pay stubs, and any other paperwork specific to the chosen loan type. Having these items organized streamlines the application process and demonstrates preparedness to lenders. Key tip: Keep digital and physical copies of all submitted materials for future reference. ### Step 4: Check Eligibility Criteria Familiarize yourself with the eligibility criteria set forth by the selected lender. Common factors include minimum credit score thresholds, debt-to-income ratios, employment history, and collateral availability. Ensuring compliance early on reduces the risk of unnecessary rejections or delays. Guideline: Address any discrepancies or weaknesses in advance, either by improving your credit score, reducing debts, or seeking cosigners or guarantors if permitted. ### Step 5: Calculate Affordability Okay would you look at this one next? Greek gods play a large role in the myth of how the Trojan War was started. The reason gods were introduced to the Greek culture was to serve as an answer to the origin of man, as well as to offer authority figures that could be summoned when the Greek needed help. The Greek gods were believed to have a divine presence and were worshipped by many. They were an important aspect of Greek culture that flowed over into literature, art, and other topics. Many of Homer's works include the involvement of Greek gods and goddesses. The gods that are seen throughout Homer's works are immortal and they hold a lot of power. They have the power to know just about everything and are the highest in authority without any other power above them. The greatest of the gods is the family in which Zeus is the father. Throughout the majority of Homer's writings, Zeus is so supreme that he is used to represent the collective power of gods and Zeus is so commonly mentioned. This is why in the origin of the Trojan War, the gods go to Zeus for resolutions of conflict. Unlike some works of writing and some cultures, the gods throughout Homer's work have no relation to religion but are rather their own separate thing. They have ridges on their trunks which end in two points. Title: How to Change Your Screen Resolution in Windows 7 Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through changing your screen resolution in Windows 7. Adjusting the screen resolution can help improve readability, better fit content to your monitor size, or optimize visual quality depending on the task at hand. By following these steps, you'll ensure that text and graphics are displayed clearly, making your computing experience more enjoyable and efficient. Key Terms: - Display settings: The configuration options related to how information is presented on your computer screen. - Monitor: A device connected to your computer that displays visual output. - Screen resolution: Refers to the number of pixels (tiny dots) used to display an image on your monitor; measured as width x height. - DPI (dots per inch): Measurement of pixel density within a specific area. Guide: 1. Access Display Settings: Click the Start button (Windows icon), located at the bottom left corner of the screen, then select Control Panel from the list provided. Once in the Control Panel, navigate to Appearance and Personalization > Display. Alternatively, right-click anywhere on the desktop and choose 'Screen Resolution.' This action directly opens the Display window without going through the Control Panel route. 2. Understanding the Display Window: Upon opening the Display window, you're greeted with several tabs containing different customizable features of your monitor setup. For this tutorial, focus on the 'Resolution' section under the 'Change the appearance of your display' option. Here, you see two dropdown boxes labeled 'Resolution:' - one represents horizontal pixels while the other shows vertical pixels. Higher values mean sharper but smaller items, whereas lower values make them larger yet less sharp. 3. Identify Current Resolution: Check what value is currently selected since this gives you an idea about the default setting chosen by either yourself or the system manufacturer during installation. If you haven't changed anything before, chances are it would be set to whatever suits most users based on their hardware configurations. 4. Choosing New Resolution: To change the resolution, simply click on the arrow buttons next to both boxes until you find a suitable combination that balances readability and visual fidelity according to your preference. Keep in mind that some resolutions might not work correctly due to compatibility issues between applications and your graphic card capabilities. Therefore, always opt for standardized settings when possible. 5. Testing Changes: After selecting a new resolution, hit Apply instead of OK because the former allows you to temporarily view changes before committing to them permanently. During this preview phase, observe whether any distortions occur, like stretched or squished windows, unreadable fonts, etc. If everything looks fine, proceed to confirm the alteration; otherwise, revert back to the previous state using the Revert button appearing alongside Apply after applying changes. 6. Confirming Selection: Press OK if satisfied with the adjusted resolution. Otherwise, repeat steps four and five till achieving optimal results. Remember that once confirmed, the new resolution becomes effective immediately across all open programs, so expect slight disruptions during transition periods. Tips & Guidelines: - Changing resolution affects individual program interfaces too, meaning they may need adjustments individually for best viewing experiences. - Some games require certain minimum resolutions to run smoothly, failing which could lead to suboptimal performance or crashes. - Lower resolutions consume fewer resources than higher ones, thus benefiting those running older systems or wanting extended battery life on laptops. - Monitors have native resolutions offering highest picture quality; consult manual or search online for details regarding yours. - Always restart your PC after major driver updates involving integrated or dedicated GPUs to guarantee proper functionality. Today we are going to talk about groups and something called "cocommutativity." Now don't let those big words scare you because I promise it's not as complicated as it sounds! First, let's imagine a group as a bunch of friends who like to play different games together. Each friend has a special ability or power that helps them win certain games. For example, Friend A is really good at solving puzzles while Friend B excels at climbing trees. When these friends work together, they form a group where each member brings their own unique strength to the table. Now, in mathematics, there's something called a "coproduct," which tells us how two elements in our group interact with each other. In our example, the coproduct would describe how combining the powers of Friend A and Friend B results in a new superpower (maybe they can now solve puzzles while hanging on a tree branch!). When a group has a cocommutative coproduct, it means that no matter how we combine our friends' abilities, swapping their order doesn't change the outcome. So whether Friend A helps Friend B first or vice versa, they still end up with the same combined superpower! But here comes the interesting part - when we apply this concept to a specific type of group known as a "group function Hopf algebra", things get even more intriguing! It turns out that if this Hopf algebra is "quasi-triangular," then its underlying group must be Abelian. An Abelian group is just like our friendly group but with one extra rule - every pair of members commute, meaning neither friend's ability affects or changes the other's. They can team up without worrying about affecting each other's powers. So next time you find yourself playing games with your pals, think about how exchanging partners could affect the overall gameplay, and maybe discover some hidden rules within your group dynamics! Title: How to Get Your Parents to Give You Money Introduction: This guide is designed to help you approach your parents for money in a respectful, responsible, and persuasive manner. While there's no guarantee that they will agree to give you money, following these steps can significantly increase the chances of them considering your request favorably. Remember, open communication, honesty, and demonstrating maturity are essential throughout this process. Step 1: Evaluate Your Needs Before approaching your parents, take some time to assess why you need the money. Is it for something important like school supplies or medical expenses? Or do you want to buy non-essential items such as video games or clothes? Understanding the purpose of your request will enable you to communicate effectively with your parents about your needs. Key Tip: Focusing on essential needs rather than wants may make it easier to convince your parents. Step 2: Determine an Appropriate Amount Once you have identified the reason for needing money, determine how much money you actually require. Be realistic; don't ask for more than what you genuinely need. Research prices online or consult professionals if necessary to ensure accuracy. Guideline: Overestimating might raise suspicions, while underestimating could lead to repeated requests for additional funds. Step 3: Prepare a Formal Request Write down your thoughts clearly and concisely. Explain the reasons for requiring the money, specify the amount needed, and outline when and how you plan to repay (if applicable). This shows organization, responsibility, and seriousness - qualities that adults appreciate. Key Tip: Including a repayment plan even if it isn't expected can demonstrate financial awareness and commitment. Step 4: Choose the Right Time and Place Approach your parents at a calm moment when they aren't busy or stressed. Make sure both parents are present so everyone hears the same information simultaneously. Avoid asking during arguments or disciplinary conversations. Guideline: A peaceful environment fosters clear communication and understanding. Step 5: Present Your Case Politely Speak respectfully, maintain eye contact, and be confident but humble. Use your prepared statement as a guideline but adapt it naturally to the conversation. Listen carefully to their responses and answer any questions honestly. Key Tip: Show gratitude regardless of their decision. Expressing thanks goes a long way towards building trust and rapport. Step 6: Respect Their Decision Understand that your parents may say no, and accept their response gracefully. They might have valid concerns related to family finances, personal values, or doubts about your ability to manage money wisely. If denied, consider negotiating a smaller amount or finding alternative ways to earn money yourself. Guideline: Persistence can turn into nagging quickly. Accepting 'no' without argument shows maturity and patience. Conclusion: Remember, getting your parents to give you money involves demonstrating fiscal responsibility, communicating openly, and showing appreciation. By following these steps, you show respect for your parents' authority and wisdom, which strengthens your relationship and increases the likelihood of future successful requests. Rationale: This lesson will help students identify the short vowel correspondence u=/u/. Students will learn to recognize /u/ in spoken words by learning a meaningful representation (scratching their heads, like they're confused). By using a letter box lesson, students will learn how to spell and read words containing this correspondence. Students will practice decoding /u/ words by reading a decodable book which focuses on u=/u/. Materials: Letter boxes for each student, document camera, letters for each student (b, c, f, G, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, s, t, u), flashcards with (slush, fun, cut, sub, Gus, truck, jump, luck, slump). Bud the Sub, assessment worksheet (URL below). 1. Say: Our written language is like a code. In order to be the best readers we can be, we have to learn how to read the code. We have to learn how each letter sounds. Today we're going to learn about short u. When I say /u/, I think of a person who is confused and doesn't know the answer. Have you ever been asked a question that you didn't know and said /u/. Well every time we hear that sound I want you to think of the letter u, and I want you to scratch your head like you are confused and don't know the answer. 2. Before we start trying to spell /u/ words, we need to listen for it in some words. When I listen for /u/ in words, I think of that confused person, saying "Uhhh" (scratch your head). When I say /u/, I feel my jaw drop and my mouth open. For example, when I say cup, I hear /u/ like the confused person says, and I feel my mouth open up and my jaw drop down. Now I am going to see if /u/ is in cute. I didn't hear /u/ in cute. My jaw didn't drop and my mouth made more of a kissy face than opening up. Now let's all try. I want you to scratch your head like the confused person if you hear /u/ in a word. If you don't hear /u/, then don't scratch your head. Do you hear /u/ in cut? Paste? Walk? Run? Jump? Skip? 3. I'm going to show you how to spell the words for our letter boxes. We are going to use the word slush. "The snow had melted with the rain and now there was an icy slush on the roads." To spell slush in letterboxes, we need to recognize the different sounds we hear in the word. Lay out four boxes for your letterbox on the document camera to model, because this word will have four sounds, we have to stretch it out to hear those sounds. Sssllluuush. I heard the /u/ right before that last sound /sh/, so let's put that U in the third letterbox. Then let's look at the beginning of the word. First I heard that /s/, and we know that sound is made by s. Then I heard the /l/ sound. That sound is made by the letter l. So now we have /s//l//u/ and all we have left is that one sound /sh/. Now remember, even though it is only one sound, doesn't mean it is only one letter. What makes that /sh/ sound? It is the letters sh. If I were to encounter this word in my reading and I didn't know how to say it, I would first look at the /u/ because we know it makes that "Uhh" confused sound. Then I would go to the beginning of the word and stretch out the sounds of each of those letters. In slush, I would go ssslll, there's that "uhhh". Then finally I would move on to the last sound, that sshh. Slluuusssh, slush! That's it! "There was slush on the road that made it dangerous to drive." 4. Now you are going to try and spell some words in the letterboxes. We are going to start with 3 boxes, and your word is fun. "It is fun to play games with my friends." Do you hear that confused /u/ somewhere in run? Good, that's right, it's in the middle! So now we stretch out the word. What do you think goes in the first box? Right! You heard /f/ and knew that f went in the first box. Ok, so now you just have the last box. N, good job! (Make sure to take a quick mental record, making sure you check that each student is following along and assessing who is understanding the task). Ok, the next word is cut. "I cut my finger on the sharp knife". (Follow the same procedure as above for this word as well as sub, Gus, truck, jump, luck, and slump.) 5. Good work everyone! I like the way you all were able to identify that /u/ sound in your reading words! Now we are going to read a story called Bud the Sub. This book is about a submarine, can anyone tell me what a submarine is? That's right, its like a ship that is made to go underwater. Well this sub's name is Bud. And bud has a job to help other ships that are in trouble. Bud learns of one ship that has been hit! Let's see what Bud can do to help. Do you think he'll be able to save the ship? You are going to get with a partner and buddy read to find out what Bud does. (Students buddy read as the teacher goes around to check the student's reading. After everyone finishes, the teachers reads Bud the Sub to the class and stops before she turns each page to ask questions, talk about the plot and review u=/u/ words.) 6. For assessment, the students will be given a handout. Say: Each student is going to work independently on this worksheet. I want to see what you learned today! On the sgeet there are some pictures with words written in. I want you to color the pictures that have that short u, /u/ sound. You should not color the pictures that don't have that /u/ sound in the words. Are there any questions before you begin? No? Okay then you can start. I will collect the worksheets when you are done. Title: How to Draw a Farmer Drawing a farmer can be both fun and challenging! This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating your own farmer illustration with simple steps and helpful tips along the way. By breaking down the drawing into basic shapes, then gradually refining them, even beginners can create an impressive piece of art. Let's get started! **Step 1:** Sketching Basic Shapes Start by lightly sketching two intersecting lines that form a cross shape. These lines serve as guides for positioning and proportionating the body parts accurately. The vertical line indicates the center of the figure while the horizontal one divides the upper and lower halves. Next, draw circles at the intersection points - small ones for the head (about 1/8th of the total height), larger ones for major joint areas such as shoulders, hips, knees, and ankles. Add ovals for arms and legs. Make sure these shapes align roughly with your initial guideline. ![Sketched Guidelines](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Draw-a-Person-Step-1-Version-3.jpg/aid475260-v4- Daddy when I go up I want Panther. Today, let's talk about a fun way to describe collections of numbers using some special symbols. This will help us understand the difference between two types of numbers - real numbers (which include all decimals) and natural numbers (the counting numbers: 1, 2, 3, ...). Imagine you have a set of number cards with dots on them representing each number. Real number cards go from zero to infinity, including decimal points, while natural number cards start from 1 and also go up to infinity. Let's call these sets by their special names: for real numbers and for natural numbers. Now, we want to create short "codes" or "shorthand" for groups of these numbers, like when you group similar toys together. We'll make our codes using the "power" symbol (^), parentheses ( ), and the vertical bar (|). These codes are called Set Builder Notation, and here's how they work: * ** ^>(a)** means "all real numbers greater than 'a'". So if I pick 'a' as 5, my code becomes ^>(5), which represents all those number cards having values greater than 5. * ** ^( a)** stands for "all real numbers except 'a'", so ^( 7) has every number card excluding 7. * ** ^+** signifies "positive natural numbers", meaning all the natural number cards starting from 1, given by ^(>0). Finally, we get to create shorthands for specific ranges of real and natural numbers! Our new codes should still begin with or but now followed by square brackets [ ] around the range of numbers: * ** [[0,1]]** is the collection of all real numbers between 0 and 1, inclusive - just like your number line hanging in class! * ** [[1,w]]** shows the set containing the first 'w' natural numbers; for example, if 'w' were 4, then [[1,4]] would represent {1, 2, 3, 4}. These new codes give us an easy and clear method to write down different collections of numbers while showing whether we're talking about real or natural ones. Have fun creating more combinations and sharing them with friends! Remember, math is everywhere, even in finding clever ways to express ourselves. Happy exploring! A mommy kangaroo is called a doe and a baby kangaroo is called a joey. Can you see this kangaroo's large ears? Well, you guessed it! They have excellent hearing and can swivel their large ears in all directions to pick up sounds. Let's have a little hearing test today. If you were in trouble, and you needed to shout "help", would you shout really loud? Let's hear you? Can everyone hear that? Now let's have everyone whisper the same thing. Ready? One, two three! How was that? Could you still hear that? How about if we just think the word "help". Let's see what that's like. One the count of three, everyone think "help" as hard as you can. Ready? One, two, three. Did anyone hear that? Why not? How do you think God's hearing is? Do you think He could hear us when we whispered? How about when we just thought the word "help"? What do you think was easier for God to hear - the yell or the thought? I have some good news for you. God has better hearing than we do and even better hearing than a kangaroo! He heard us yell for help, he heard us whisper it, and he even heard us think it. All three times God could hear us just as if we'd spoken right in His ear. Psalm 4:3 says, "The Lord will hear when I call to him." God hears and answers our call because He cares about us. Title: How to Check Login Details of Your Gmail Account Introduction: In this comprehensive guide, we will walk you through the process of checking your login details for your Gmail account. By understanding and regularly verifying these details, you can ensure that your account remains secure and protected from unauthorized access. This is especially crucial if you suspect any suspicious activity or want to maintain optimal privacy settings. Follow these steps carefully and keep track of important information as needed. Step 1: Opening Google Account Settings Visit <https://myaccount.google.com/> using your preferred web browser (Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Safari, etc.). Sign in with your Gmail address and password when prompted. It's essential to remember your credentials because they are required to manage your account settings. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use strong, unique passwords consisting of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters. Avoid reusing old passwords or sharing them with others. - Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) by clicking the 'Security' tab later in this guide. With 2FA enabled, even if someone knows your password, they cannot log into your account without also having access to your registered phone number. Step 2: Access Security Overview Once logged in, click on the 'Security' tab located at the left sidebar under 'Personal info'. Here, you will find an overview of various security features associated with your Gmail account. You may need to scroll down slightly depending on your screen size. Step 3: Review Recent Activity Under the 'Your devices' section, review the list of recent sign-in activities along with their respective locations, dates, times, device types, and browsers used. If there are unfamiliar entries, consider changing your password immediately and enabling two-factor authentication. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Pay close attention to IP addresses; sometimes, cities might be incorrectly identified due to VPN usage or similar services. Focus more on unusual countries or regions instead. - Regularly monitor your account activity to detect potential breaches early on. Report any suspicious behavior directly to Google via the provided support channels. Step 4: Manage Device Access Below the recent activity list, check all authorized devices connected to your Gmail account. Remove those no longer needed or recognized. Unfamiliar devices could indicate unauthorized access attempts. Step 5: Verify Linked Email Addresses and Phone Numbers Scroll further down until you reach the 'Ways we can verify it's you' section. Make sure only trusted email addresses and phone numbers appear here since they serve as verification methods during recovery processes. Delete any unknown or unwanted contacts listed. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Keep one primary personal email separate from your work or school accounts for added security. - Add multiple trustworthy contact points so you can recover your account quickly should issues arise. Step 6: Assess Third-Party Access Permissions Continue exploring other sections within the 'Security' tab, such as 'Third-party access', where you can view applications granted permission to interact with your Gmail data. Revoke authorization for unnecessary apps to minimize risks. Conclusion: By following these thorough steps, you have now successfully checked your Gmail account's login details, ensuring maximum security against unauthorized intrusions. Remember to frequently review your account settings, monitor activity logs, update passwords periodically, and utilize available tools like two-factor authentication for enhanced protection. Stay vigilant and enjoy peace of mind knowing your digital communications remain private and controlled exclusively by you. No I want you to sit down Sis. I know you are but what am I...
