Turning Variables into Numbers, Part II This is episode two about turning variables into numbers, you should start this one if you're sure you really understand the basics and ready to build on it with this episode. First of all let's start out with a recap of what the last episode was all about. Turning variables into numbers has three general steps, first of all you assign numbers to your variables, then based on those numbers you solve the problem and lastly you look for the match between your answer choices and the answer that you found. Now we're going to add two more wrinkles to turning variables into numbers. First of all if answer choices include variables which we haven't seen so far, so far we've only seen answer choices that had numbers, then you're going to turn those variables into numbers as well. Secondly if more than one answer choice works then you're going to need to go back, change your numbers and go through the process again until you only have one answer choice that continues to work. Let's look at all this in more details using some examples. So here we have an example of the first new principle which is that sometimes you have to turn variables in the answer choices into numbers as well, but we're still going to go through the same three basic steps so let's do that. Let's start by reading the problem now, 'if a, b and c are integers greater than one with ab=15 and bc=33, which of the following must be true?' So the first step is to choose numbers that work and remember you have to meet all the restrictions in the problems so choose carefully. Now you might naturally say okay if A times B is 15 maybe we'll try three for A and five for B. But one thing that makes this one tricky is that since this B is five, this B also has to be five and yet there's no integer for C that would work, five times nothing is going to give you 33, at least not given that C is an integer. So sometimes you have to back up and try again so this three and five combination must be wrong, so let's start over. Instead of three times five, let's try five times three that will fix our problem. We still have five times three as fifteen so that's good and yet now that B is three, we're going to be able to make this statement true. Three times what is 33? Three times 11. So we've beat all the restrictions in the problem and we're ready to solve the question. The question is which of the following must be true? So actually we kind of have to look at the answer choices in order to solve the problem and that's where we get to our new guideline which is that sometimes variables have to be turned into numbers. For instance to know whether B is greater than A is greater than C is true, got to put the numbers in. So let's do that what was B? B was three, so three is greater than A, that was five is greater than C which was 11, not so much three is not greater than either of those, so we'll get rid of that one. B; A is greater than C is greater than B, A is five is greater than C which is 11, we can actually quit here 'cause five is not greater than 11. C; C is greater than A that's 11 which is greater than A which is five and is that greater than B which is three? Yes. We've found our answer so we can quit now. So the principle here is that sometimes you have to take your answer choices and also make them numerical, if they're not already numerical. Let's look at one more example of that idea. 'If Q=rs which of the following must be equal to qs?' So yet again we need to pick some numbers and here's a little tip, if we pick Q first it might be a little harder to find R and S that are easy to deal with so we should pick R and S first and then have that determine what Q has to be. Also in general we want to avoid the numbers one and two, so let's try three for R and four for S and then three times four that makes Q 12. So we've already picked numbers and we're ready to do step two, answering the question. The question is basically what does QS equal so let's do that. Q is 12 and S is four so what is QS equal? 48 so that's our answer and we're ready to look for a match among the choices but wait they're variables not numbers so we need to turn them into numbers before we can find the match. So let's do all the plugging in. R squared times S is three squared times four that's nine times four which is 36 and that's not a match for 48. B; Q squared over R Q squared is 12 squared that's 144 divided by R which is three and that does turn out to be 48. Now it's up to you at this point whether you think that you want to quit because you usually will not find more than one match but just to be safe you should generally keep on going. So we tentatively think that this is our answer but to be safe, let's do the rest. C is R over Q that's three twelfths that's a very small fraction nowhere close to 48, so we're done. D is R squared over S that's three squared over four that's also a relatively small fraction, nowhere near forty eight it's out and S squared over R that is four squared which is 16 over R which is three, still a relatively small number definitely not 48. And so we've confirmed that the right answer is indeed B. So there's that strategy but let's look at the other one right? That was that sometimes you're going to find more than one correct answer and remember when that happens you go back and change your answers and you keep on doing that until you've narrowed it down to one answer choice that is always right no matter what numbers you choose. So let's try this one; 'if zero is less than x is less than 1 and -1 is less than y is less than zero which of the following must be true? So let's start by choosing a value, let's try X equals a quarter and Y equals negative, let's see one half. Okay so is it true that X squared is greater than Y squared? Well this is going to square to one sixteenth this is going to square to one forth, one sixteenth is smaller so this is out sounds good to me. Now what about X minus Y is greater than zero? One quarter minus negative one half is one quarter plus a half which ends up being three quarters, is three quarters greater than zero? Yes it is so we're going to have to leave B in, it might be the right answer or it might just work with these particular numbers. Let's try C; Y minus X is greater than zero so that would be negative one half minus a quarter that's negative three quarters that's not greater than zero, so we eliminate that one. D, x+y is greater than zero, that's one quarter plus negative one half that's negative a quarter not greater than zero, so that's out. Looks like we're doing pretty good let's see how E treats us x+y is less than zero, well we just did that one right? And it was negative a quarter, so negative a quarter is less than zero also true, so we actually have it narrowed down to two choices. So we don't actually know yet what the right answer is but we can pick new values for X and Y and again see which of the two answer choices continues to work no matter what. So let's mix it up a little before the size of X was smaller than the size of Y, technically I should be using the word magnitude. So let's mix that up, let's try making X into one half, still positive 'cause it has to be and making Y negative one quarter still a negative 'cause it has to be and then let's see how that impacts B and E. This time x-y is one half minus negative a quarter that ends up being three quarters and it's greater than zero sounds good, still true. Down here let's see if this looks true x+y is less than zero, one half plus negative a quarter well that's one quarter less than zero? No longer true, so we've managed to eliminate E, B remains true so we've found our answer. There's more than one correct answer at first but by changing numbers we were able to narrow it down to just one right answer. Let's look at one more example of this idea, 'if -1 is less than n is less than 1 and N is not equal to zero which of the following must be true? So let's pick a number let's try N equals a half. We do have to make sure that we're between negative one and one, a lot of people naturally pick integers so we'll try one or two or three but that clearly violates the rule so watch out for that. Now let's see what's true is it true that negative one half is less than one half? Yeah that works so we leave A in. Is it true that one half is less than one half squared which is a quarter? Nope one half is actually bigger so B is out. Is it true that these are just reversed is it true that one quarter is less than one half? That's true so we're going to leave C in as an option. N cubed that's going to one eighth, is it true that one eighth is less than N squared that's one half squared which is a quarter, yap an eighth is less than a quarter so we'll leave that in. And E is it true that one half is less than one half cubed which is one eighth, nope that's out too. So as you can still see we still have quite a bit of work to do. Let's mix it up and this time since we tried a positive fraction let's try a negative fraction we want a different kind of number. But to keep it simple we can stick with negative one half 'cause we know how to deal with one halves efficiently. Looking at A, negative N is now equal to one half and is that less than N, negative one half? No A is out now and I want to point out that we often think of negative signs as meaning that we're dealing with a negative number but that's not true. In A, with this value of N that negative N just means switch the sign so instead of meaning it's a negative number it means it becomes a positive number kind of unexpected. C; N squared is negative one half squared and that's a quarter is that less than N which is negative one half? No a negative number is not bigger it's actually smaller so C is out. So it looks like D is the right answer but let's just verify N cubed is negative one eighth because remember negative numbers Jim asks: "Knowing that water and moisture should always weigh the same, why do clouds form at different altitudes? What makes a cirrus cloud different from a stratus cloud?" Clouds are grouped into three different levels in the sky based on altitude. Low-level clouds, which include cumulus and stratus clouds, are 6,500 feet or below. Mid-level clouds are between 6,500 and 23,000 feet. High-level clouds, like cirrus clouds, are between 16,500 and 45,000 feet high. As for stratus clouds and cirrus clouds, they're both one of three basic shapes of clouds. Stratus is Latin for "layer". Those clouds form in layers and often cover the entire sky. They're much wider than they are thick, and they kind of look like a blanket or a mattress. Another way to describe the way they look is a layer of fog that never reaches the ground. In fact, fog that lifts from the ground is actually a layer of low stratus clouds. Cirrus is Latin for "curl" because those are clouds of wispy curls. Some people think they look like a child's hair. Those clouds usually form about 18,000 feet and are thin because they form in higher levels of atmosphere where little water vapor is present. The third type of cloud, cumulus, is Latin for "heap". They're basically heaps of separated cloud masses. They have flat bottoms and cauliflower tops. Parts of the Sentence - Subject/Verb A simple sentence is a group of words expressing a complete thought, and it must have a subject and a verb (predicate - some grammar books use the word predicate, but I will use verb). A verb shows action or state of being. Examples: The bell rang. The boy is here. The subject tells who or what about the verb. Examples: The bell rang. The boy is here. There are four (4) kinds of sentences: declarative, imperative, interrogative, and exclamatory. 1. A declarative sentence makes a statement. Example: The assignment is due tomorrow. 2. An imperative sentence gives a command or makes a request. Examples: Hand it in now. Stop. 3. An interrogative sentence asks a question. Example: Do you know the man? 4. An exclamatory sentence shows strong feeling. Declarative, imperative, or interrogative sentences can be made into exclamatory sentences by punctuating them with an exclamation point. Examples: The assignment is due tomorrow! Stop! Do you know the man! When finding the subject and the verb in a sentence, always find the verb first and then say who or what followed by the verb. Example: The bell rang. Find the verb - rang. Now say who or what rang? The bell rang. Bell is the subject. Imperative sentences always have an understood but not stated you as the subject. Examples: Hand it in now. (You) hand it in now. Stop. (You) stop. Instructions: Find the subject and verb in the following sentences. 1. Give me the gun! 2. Help me please. 3. Bring me the paper. 4. Lock the car door always. 5. Run next door for some sugar. --For answers scroll down. 1. (you) - subject, give - verb 2. (you) - subject, help - verb 3. (you) - subject, bring - verb 4. (you) - subject, lock - verb 5. (you) - subject, run - verb DAILY GRAMMAR - - - - by Mr. Johanson Copyright 2012 Word Place, Inc - - All Rights Reserved. MORAL. Double dealing is always detestable. The man that blows hot and cold at the same time is not worthy to be trusted; the sooner we part from him the better. FABLE LXIV. Title: How to Get the Glide Ability in Kingdom Hearts 2 Kingdom Hearts 2 is an action role-playing game that combines the elements of hack and slash and button mashing. The game features various abilities that Sora, the main character, can learn throughout his journey. One such ability is Glide, which allows Soria to glide through the air using his Keyblade like a makeshift hang glider. This guide will show you how to get the Glide ability in Kingdom Hearts 2. **Step 1: Progress Through the Story Until You Reach Neverland** The Glide ability becomes available during the story's progression when Sora reaches Neverland. Specifically, this occurs after completing the Hollow Bastion portion of the game but before confronting Xemnas at the World That Never Was. It is essential to complete certain missions and objectives leading up to this point to unlock the necessary events required to obtain the Glide ability. *Key Tip:* Save your game regularly, especially if you are playing on Critical Mode or Proud Mode, as these difficulty levels increase the challenge significantly. **Step 2: Complete the Mission "Clear the Threat" in Neverland** In Neverland, Peter Pan tasks Sora with clearing out a group of Heartless infesting Skull Rock. Completing this mission advances the plot and triggers additional cutscenes, ultimately resulting in obtaining the Glide ability. To clear the threat: 1. Head to Skull Rock via the galleon docked near the Lost Boys hideout. 2. Enter the cave entrance on the left side of Skull Rock. 3. Defeat all the enemies inside the cavern area. 4. Exit the cave and defeat more groups of enemies outside until reaching the final battle against Captain Hook. 5. After defeating Captain Hook, return to the Lost Boys hideout to trigger further cutscenes and receive the Glide ability from Tinker Bell. *Key Guideline:* Keep an eye on your HP (health points) during battles, ensuring you have enough health potions or ethers to heal yourself when needed. **Step 3: Equip the Glide Ability** After acquiring the Glide ability, make sure to equip it by accessing the menu screen. Follow these steps to do so: 1. Open the Main Menu by pressing the Start Button. 2. Select "Equip." 3. Choose either Command Deck A or B, depending on where you want to place the Glide ability. 4. Scroll down to find the newly acquired Glide ability and select it. 5. Press the Confirm Button to assign Glide to one of the shortcut command slots. Now that Glide has been equipped, press the assigned button during combat or exploration to activate the ability, giving Sora the freedom to explore new heights! *Key Tips:* Remember that Glide consumes MP (Mana Points), so be mindful of its usage. Also, combining Glide with other movement abilities like Dodge Roll creates unique traversal options. You know what the mother lion's called? Is that the wave or is this the wave? Extracting files from an archive online is a useful skill that can save you time and hassle when dealing with compressed files. One popular web-based tool for extracting archives is B1.org. This tutorial will provide a detailed guide on how to use this service to extract files from an archive online. Step 1: Go to the B1.org website Open your preferred web browser and navigate to the B1.org homepage (<https://b1.org/>). The page should have a simple interface with options to upload or drag and drop your archive file. Key tip: Make sure you are connected to a stable internet connection before proceeding as large files may take some time to upload. Step 2: Upload the Archive File Click on the blue "SelectArchive" button located at the center of the screen to open the file explorer window. Navigate through your computer's directories until you find the archive file you wish to extract. Once selected, click 'Open'. Alternatively, you can drag and drop the archive directly into the designated area. Guideline: Only .zip, .rar, .7z, .arj, .tar, .gzip, .bzip2, .iso, .cab, .chm, .deb, .dmg, .gz, .hfs, .jar, .msi, .pkg, .rpm, .war, .xar formats are supported by B1.org. If your archive uses a different format, consider using alternative software or converting the file first. Step 3: Wait for the File to Upload Once you've chosen the archive, wait for it to fully upload onto B1.org servers. You can monitor its progress via the status bar below the 'Select Archive' button. Please note that larger files might require more time to complete the transfer process. Step 4: Choose Extraction Location After successful upload, select the destination folder where you want the extracted files to be saved. To do so, click on the 'Browse' button next to the text field labeled 'Save to'. A new window will appear prompting you to choose a location. Select any directory within your system then hit 'OK'. Step 5: Start Extracting Files With both the source archive file and extraction path set up, press the green 'Extract' button found near the bottom right corner of the page. Depending on the size of the archive, this could take anywhere from seconds to several minutes. Key Tip: Avoid closing the tab or navigating away from the site during the extraction process as doing so might interrupt the operation leading to incomplete extractions. Step 6: Verify Successful Extraction Upon completion, a confirmation message along with a link to download the log report will pop up. Click 'Download Log' if needed but otherwise simply close the tab knowing that your files were successfully extracted. Guideline: Check the target folder immediately after extraction to ensure all intended files were correctly decompressed without errors or missing content. By following these steps meticulously, users should now feel comfortable utilizing B1.org to extract various types of archived files conveniently over the internet. Remember always to maintain good cybersecurity practices while sharing data online! Title: How to Disable iOS 7 Animations Disabling animations in iOS 7 can help improve your device's performance, especially if you have an older model or are running multiple apps simultaneously. This tutorial will guide you through the process of disabling various animations found in iOS 7, providing clear instructions and explanations along the way. **Step 1:** Accessing Settings Begin by locating and tapping on the **Settings** app, which is typically represented by grey gears on a white background. The Settings app allows users to customize their devices according to personal preferences, such as adjusting display settings, managing storage, and enabling parental controls. **Step 2:** Navigating to General Settings Within the Settings app, scroll down until you find the section labeled **General**. Tap on this option to proceed. Here, you will be able to modify general system-wide settings like accessibility features, software updates, and keyboard options. **Step 3:** Enabling Reduce Motion Under the General Settings menu, locate and select the option titled **Accessibility**. Inside Accessibility, look for the subsection named **Motion**. Within this category, toggle on the switch next to **Reduce Motion**. By doing so, you will disable several visual effects that occur during transitions between screens, making navigation smoother and quicker. Key Tips: * When Reduce Motion is enabled, certain animations such as parallax (the illusion of movement created when tilting or moving your device) and zoom effects while opening and closing apps will no longer appear. * It is important to note that turning off all motion may cause some elements within specific applications to become less responsive or difficult to interact with due to their reliance on these animations for proper functionality. **Step 4:** Adjusting Transparency and Brightness Still under the Accessibility settings, navigate to the **Increase Contrast** subcategory. Turn on both the switches beside **Reduce Transparency** and **Darken Colors**. These two settings work together to reduce transparency throughout the operating system, improving overall readability and reducing strain on your device's resources. Key Guidelines: * Reducing transparency can make text easier to read against backgrounds and decrease eye fatigue over extended usage periods. * Darkening colors provides better contrast for those who struggle with low vision or require additional assistance when reading small text. **Step 5:** Managing Auto-Lock Timeout Return to the main Settings page and tap on **Display & Brightness**. Under Display Zoom, ensure that your device is set to Standard view instead of Zoomed. Afterward, locate the heading **Auto-Lock**, which determines how quickly your screen turns off after being idle. Set this value as short as possible without causing inconvenience; setting it too high could lead to unnecessary battery consumption. **Step 6:** Controlling Background App Refresh Navigate back to the main Settings page and choose **General** once more. Scroll down until you see **Background App Refresh**. Toggle off any apps that do not need constant updating in real time. This feature allows selected applications to fetch new data even when they aren't actively open, consuming valuable processing power and potentially slowing down your device. By following these steps, you should now have successfully disabled many of the animations present in iOS 7, resulting in improved performance and reduced resource utilization. Remember, individual needs may vary, so feel free to experiment with different combinations of settings to find what works best for you! No I can't, I thought I could make a flower. [SIB steps into the kitchen CHI points to a place] Title: How to Gracefully Handle Rudeness from Luxury Store Employees Introduction: Shopping at luxury stores can be an exciting experience, but sometimes you may encounter rude behavior from employees. This guide aims to equip you with effective strategies to handle such situations while maintaining your dignity and composure. Remember, rudeness is often a reflection of the employee's attitude or personal issues rather than your worth as a customer. Here are some steps to help you deal with luxury stores being rude: Step 1: Stay Calm and Composed When faced with rudeness, take a deep breath and maintain your calmness. Allowing yourself to become angry or upset will only escalate the situation and possibly reflect poorly on you. By staying composed, you demonstrate maturity and class, which might even prompt the employee to reconsider their behavior. Key Tip: Practice mindfulness techniques before shopping to help manage stress and remain calm during potentially frustrating encounters. Step 2: Analyze the Situation Try to understand if the rudeness was intentional or unintentional. Sometimes, language barriers, miscommunication, or simple mistakes can lead to perceived rudeness. If you determine that the employee did indeed act impolitely, move on to Step 3. Guideline: Give the benefit of the doubt; assume good intentions until proven otherwise. Step 3: Address the Behavior Politely Speak softly yet assertively to express your concern about the employee's behavior. Use phrases like, "Excuse me, I noticed that..." or "I feel uncomfortable when..." Avoid accusatory statements and focus on addressing the specific action instead of attacking the person. Key Tip: Maintain eye contact and speak clearly to ensure effective communication. " Boy I think he needs his rain jacket today don't you Fri? The Moon and the planet Mars highlight the pre-dawn sky tomorrow. Mars looks like a bright orange star a little to the upper left of the Moon. They're well up in the east at first light. Mars has a couple of moons of its own, although they look nothing like our Moon. They're much smaller, and they're lumpy. Phobos, the closer of the two, looks only about a third as wide as the Moon does from Earth. It moves around Mars so quickly that it rises in the west and sets in the east. And from the planet's polar regions, it never climbs into view at all. Deimos is visible from the entire planet, but it's too small to see any detail on its surface. Instead, it looks like a brilliant star. It moves across the sky slowly, so it stays in view for more than two-and-a-half days, then disappears for the same amount of time. Those aren't the only differences you'd see in Mars's night sky. Because the planet's atmosphere is less than one percent as thick as Earth's, the stars wouldn't twinkle as much as they do in our own sky. And the light of the small moons wouldn't create as much glare as our moon does, so you could view faint stars even when both moons were in the sky. You wouldn't have the pesky glow of artificial lights, either, leaving dark skies all the time. And a bright double "star" would sometimes highlight the sky before sunrise or after sunset, one shining bright blue, the other white and much fainter: Earth and the Moon. Title: How to Cook Fully Cooked Smoked Sausage Introduction: Smoked sausage is a type of pre-cooked sausage that has been smoked at a low temperature for an extended period of time. This process enhances its flavor and gives it a firm texture. Since fully cooked smoked sausages are already cooked, they only need to be heated before serving. Here's a simple guide on how to cook fully cooked smoked sausage, ensuring perfect results every time. Things You'll Need: * Fully cooked smoked sausage (such as kielbasa or andouille) * Skillet or grill pan * Nonstick cooking spray * Tongs * Cutting board * Knife * Optional seasoning (e.g., garlic powder, paprika, black pepper, etc.) Step 1: Choose Your Smoked Sausage Purchase high-quality fully cooked smoked sausage from your local grocery store or butcher. Popular varieties include kielbasa, andouille, Polish sausage, or beef smoked sausage. Make sure you have enough for everyone. Step 2: Preheat Your Pan Place a skillet or grill pan over medium heat. Allow it to warm up for about 3 minutes. A hot surface ensures even browning and prevents sticking. Use a nonstick pan if available, but if not, apply some nonstick cooking spray to prevent sticking. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Medium heat provides optimal cooking conditions without burning the outside while leaving the inside raw. * Avoid using high heat, which can cause uneven cooking and potentially burn the exterior. Step 3: Add the Sausage Once the pan is adequately heated, add the smoked sausage links. Space them out evenly so they aren't touching one another. This allows all sides to brown properly. Step 4: Brown Each Side Using tongs, rotate the sausages occasionally until they develop a nice golden-brown color on all sides. This should take approximately 5-7 minutes per side depending on their size. Browning adds flavor through caramelization and creates an appetizing appearance. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Don't rush this step - patience pays off with better taste and texture. * If using multiple pans, divide the sausages between them to speed up the process. Step 5: Slice and Serve Remove the cooked smoked sausages from the pan and place them on a cutting board. Let them rest for a few moments before slicing diagonally into thick rounds or bite-sized pieces. Arrange attractively on a plate and serve immediately. Optional Step 6: Seasoning If desired, sprinkle some additional spices like garlic powder, paprika, or black pepper over the sausage slices just before serving. These enhancements boost the overall flavor profile, adding an extra layer of deliciousness to your dish. Conclusion: By following these easy steps, you now know how to cook fully cooked smoked sausage perfectly. Enjoy experimenting with various types of smoked sausages and flavors, making meals memorable and enjoyable for family and friends. But uh she's throwing all this [? Loose] stuff in his face. Have you ever wanted to make your math homework look as neat and tidy as a professional mathematician's work? Well, you can do just that by following these simple steps! We'll learn how to use "math mode" to make our symbols and numbers look like real math, and even how to create our own special displays for those important equations. #### Step 1: Entering Math Mode First things first - let's get started with math mode! When you write math, it's essential to use a special kind of formatting so that everything looks just right. This includes using a unique "math italic" font and adjusting the spacing between characters. Don't worry; entering math mode is super easy! Just surround your mathematical text with dollar signs ($). Like this: > $5 + 3 = 8$ Or, if you prefer, you can also use begin and end symbols: > \begin{math} > 5 + 3 = 8 > \end{math} Now, doesn't that look better than plain old text? Remember, anytime you want to write a mathematical symbol or formula, put it in math mode! #### Step 2: Creating Display Mode Sometimes, you may want to showcase a particular equation, making it stand out from the rest of your work. That's where display mode comes in handy! By setting up an equation in display mode, you give it its own line, add some extra space around it, and generally make it look more impressive. You can create a display mode equation using the "equation" environment, which will also automatically number your equation for you! Check it out: > \begin{equation} > E = mc^2 > \end{equation} Alternatively, if you don't need an automatic number, you can use the "displaymath" environment instead: > \begin{displaymath} > y = mx + b > \end{displaymath} Both options provide a cleaner presentation for your essential equations. Now, let's move on to something fun! #### Activity: Make Your Own Pretty Math Displays! Using what you learned, try creating some nice math displays! Here are some ideas to get you started: * Show off the Pythagorean theorem: $\begin{equation}a^2 + b^2 = c^2\end{equation}$ * Present Newton's second law: $\begin{equation}F=ma\end{equation}$ * Illustrate basic multiplication: $\begin{equation}(a+b)\times c=(a\times c)+(b\times c)\end{equation}$ Play around with various expressions until you feel comfortable creating beautiful math displays! And remember, practice makes perfect, so keep practicing! Yes they are creatures I can tell. Periodic comets visit us again and again. Non-periodic comets visit us only once. A Martian day is called a "sol", and is a little longer than an Earth day. Mars rotates in 24 hours and 37 minutes. It rotates on a tilted axis, just like the Earth does, so it has four different seasons. Of all the planets in the Solar System, the seasons of Mars are the most Earth-like, due to their similar axial tilt. The lengths of the Martian seasons are almost twice those of Earth's, as Mars's greater distance from the Sun leads to the Martian year being almost two Earth years long. " What if there's no soil? Kahoolawe is the smallest of the eight main volcanic islands of Hawaii. It is west of Maui and south of Lanai. It is roughly 11 miles long by across (). The highest point, Lua Makika, is above sea level. The island is dry because its low elevation does not cause much rain (orographic precipitation) to fall from the northeastern trade winds. It is a natural subject of self-congratulation to a man, when special knowledge, gained long ago without any forecast, turns out to afford a special inspiration in the present; and Johnson felt a new pleasure in the consciousness that he of all people in the world next to Jermyn had the most intimate knowledge of the Transome affairs. Still better--some of these affairs were secrets of Jermyn's. To act with doubleness towards a man whose conduct was double, was so near an approach to virtue that it deserved to be called by no meaner name than diplomacy. Dolch word lists, phrases, worksheets and activities. #1 Ten Dolch Words a Week Lesson Title/Subject/Grade Level/Time of Year This lesson may be used in kindergarten through grade 3 after appropriate modifications are made for the maturity of the students. For example, in kindergarten, you may want to introduce only two words at a time. The children are not expected to learn to spell the Dolch words, although many will learn to as you do the activities. State Standard Addressed Review Previous Skills - Divide the 3" by 5" cards into sets of 10, one set per child. Do not rubber-band them yet or the children will play with the rubber bands all through the lesson. - Choose 10 Dolch words to focus on for the week. Choose words from your reading series that are being introduced, or words that go along with the spelling list for the week, or words that are commonly used at your grade level. For this sample lesson, I chose 10 Dolch words that have the sound of long i. This sound is introduced about half way through first grade. Although the Dolch words are considered "sight" words (i.e.; not easily sounded out and, therefore, needing memorization), it does not hurt to point out the regular sounds in them. The 10 words for this lesson are: like, ride, five, nine, live (as in "These are live tadpoles."), white, find, kind, light, and right. - On each of the 10 pieces of 9" by 12" tag board or light colored construction paper, write one of the 10 Dolch words for the week in large, lowercase letters, using a black felt pen. Hold up one of the week's sight words on the 9" by 12" tag board card. Tape it to the chalkboard. Ask the students to find any sounds they know in the word. Ask if anyone knows the word. Tell the students the word. Use it in several sentences. Ask some students if they can use the word in a sentence. If the word has unusual sounds, explain them, such as: the ght in light and right is silent; the e at the end of like, ride, white, five, nine, and live causes the i to be a long i instead of a short i. Tell the students to use their pencils to write the word on one side of a 3" by 5" card as you write it on the board. Spell it as you go. Help the students draw a simple picture of the word, if possible. Look at and read the word several times, all together. Tell the students to turn the card over and write the word three more times in their favorite colors, using their crayons. Do this for each word in the set of 10. Give students a rubber band to hold the cards together and have them place them in their desks or in a basket. (Have them write their names on a card or two for identification.) Post the 10 large word cards in the classroom. Each day, have the students get out their set of cards and lay them out on their desks. The teacher says, "Show me 'find'," and the students find the word and hold it up in front of their chins. (You do not want them to wave the cards in the air; you want to be able to see them clearly so you know who recognized the word and who did not.) Do this so that they must recognize each word a couple of times. Create a class story with the words. Using the large chart paper or tag board, the teacher writes the story as the children use the Dolch words to create it. For example, the teacher writes as she says, "I like to..." Then she chooses a child to add a word to the sentence. A child says, "I like to ride." The teacher writes that and adds, "I like to ride a ____________ bike." She asks if anyone can put a word that makes sense in the blank. A child says, "I like to ride a white bike." The teacher fills in the word white and adds, "I will turn _________ at the corner." A child fills in the word right. The teacher writes, "I can ___________ my way." A child fills in the word find. The teacher writes, "A bike is a ____________ of transportation." A child fills in the word kind. Read the story together. Ask if anyone can spot another word that has the long i sound (bike). Ask if anyone knows what "transportation" means. Post the story in the classroom. The children should read the story together every day, as the teacher uses the pointer to point to the words. Independent practice might include: fill in the blank dittos, word searches, taking the flash cards home to practice with, etc. Make a ditto of the class story, replacing some Dolch words with blanks. Print the 10 Dolch words at the top of the ditto. The children must fill the blanks with the correct words. Prior to recess or lunch, point to each Dolch word posted in the room and have the class read them aloud. Create a Word Wall with all the words the class has learned throughout the year. You can use the wall for many activities to review the words. A phoneme is a vocal gesture that can be heard. Phonemes are defined as the vocal gestures that the child hears. Next it is important for the student to realize that the spelling of the word is determined by the order in which the phoneme appears in the word. When children begin to become aware of phonemes, reading begins to occur with more and more success. Title: How to Delete Specific Days and Sites in Internet Explorer's History Introduction: Internet Explorer is a popular web browser that stores your browsing history to make it easier for you to access previously visited websites. However, there might be instances when you want to delete specific days or sites from your browsing history due to privacy concerns or limited storage space. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting specific days and sites in Internet Explorer's history with clear instructions, helpful tips, and guidelines. Key Tips before Deleting Browsing History: 1. Be aware that once deleted, your browsing history cannot be recovered. Make sure you have saved any necessary information (e.g., usernames, passwords) prior to following this tutorial. 2. Closing all open windows of Internet Explorer ensures that no new data gets added while you are managing your existing browsing history. 3. Clearing your cache may affect some site settings like login credentials and preferences. You can choose whether to also remove cookies and files stored by add-ons during the cleaning process. 4. If you wish to keep certain frequently used websites accessible without cluttering up your history list, consider bookmarking them instead of relying solely on the browser history feature. 5. Regularly clearing your browsing history maintains good system performance as well as protects your personal data from potential threats. Step 1: Open Internet Explorer Start by launching Internet Explorer on your computer. Locate its icon either on your desktop, taskbar, start menu, or within the programs folder under Windows Accessories. Step 2: Close All Open Tabs & Windows To avoid adding more entries to your browsing history unintentionally, close all other tabs and windows except for the main one where you see the Homepage or Favorites center. Step 3: Navigate to Settings Click on the gear icon located at the top right corner of the window. A dropdown menu will appear; select 'Safety' followed by 'Delete browsing history...' which should be the second option down. Step 4: Choose What To Clear A dialog box labeled 'Delete Browsing History' will pop up. Here, you can check off what exactly you want to erase - Temporary Internet Files, Cookies, History, Download History, Form Data, Passwords, ActiveX Filtering, and Tracking Protection Data. For our purpose, ensure 'History' is checked. Optionally, you can tick others if needed. Step 5: Select Time Range Underneath these options, click on 'Preserve favorite website data'. Then click on the 'Today' button next to it to select only today's browsing data or manually specify a custom date range using the dropdown menu beside 'Select a time range to clear:' label. Step 6: Remove Selected Entries Now press the 'Delete' button at the bottom right corner of the dialog box. It takes a moment to complete depending upon the amount of data being removed. Once done, a confirmation message appears stating 'Browsing history was successfully deleted.' Click OK to dismiss it. Step 7: Verify Deleted History Go back to the browser window and hit Ctrl + H simultaneously to bring up the View By Date section of the History panel. Check if the specified dates or URLs have been cleared out accordingly. Conclusion: Congratulations! You have now learned how to effectively manage your Internet Explorer browsing history, ensuring greater control over your online activity records and enhancing overall security. Remember to regularly clean up your browsing data to maintain optimal system performance and safeguard sensitive information. Title: How to Choose a Marriage Counselor Introduction: Marriage counseling can be an effective way to address issues within a marriage and improve communication between partners. However, choosing the right marriage counselor is crucial for achieving positive results. This tutorial will guide you through the process of selecting a qualified and suitable marriage counselor. Step 1: Identify Your Needs and Goals Determine what specific issues you would like to work on during counseling sessions. Common areas include communication difficulties, financial stressors, infidelity, or intimacy problems. Having clear goals will help ensure that your chosen therapist has experience addressing these concerns. Key Tip: Be honest with yourself about any underlying personal issues (e.g., depression) that may also need attention during therapy. Step 2: Research Local Therapists Use online directories such as Psychology Today, GoodTherapy, or the American Association for Marriage and Family Therapy (AAMFT) to search for licensed therapists specializing in marriage counseling near you. Read their profiles carefully, focusing on education, credentials, approach to treatment, and client testimonials. Guideline: Look for certifications from reputable organizations like AAMFT or the International Centre for Excellence in Emotionally Focused Therapy (ICEEFT). These indicate additional training beyond basic licensure requirements. Step 3: Schedule Initial Consultations Most therapists offer free initial consultations where you can discuss your needs, ask questions, and determine if there's a good fit. Prepare a list of queries beforehand, including topics related to their therapeutic methods, session structure, availability, and fees. Key Tip: Pay attention to how comfortable you feel discussing sensitive matters with the potential counselor. Trust your instincts - if something feels off, consider looking elsewhere. Step 4: Evaluate Compatibility with Theoretical Orientation Different therapists subscribe to various theoretical orientations influencing their techniques and interventions. Some common ones are cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), emotionally focused therapy (EFT), solution-focused brief therapy (SFBT), and narrative therapy. Familiarize yourself with these approaches and assess which one aligns best with your preferences and objectives. Step 5: Consider Practical Aspects Take into account factors such as location, scheduling flexibility, insurance coverage, and cost when making your decision. While finding a highly skilled therapist should be your priority, logistical details play a significant role in ensuring regular attendance at appointments. Step 6: Check References and Reviews If possible, speak with past clients of the prospective counselor to gain insights into their experiences and outcomes. Online reviews can provide valuable feedback but remember to interpret them cautiously due to potential biases. Step 7: Make an Informed Decision Based on all gathered information, select the marriage counselor who appears most capable of helping you reach your relationship goals while being compatible with your personality and circumstances. Remember that trust and openness form essential components of successful therapy; thus, establishing rapport early on sets the stage for productive conversations moving forward. Conclusion: Selecting a competent and well-suited marriage counselor requires careful consideration and research. By following this comprehensive guide, couples can increase their chances of finding a knowledgeable and empathetic professional equipped to support them in navigating life's challenges together. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Brown/Sarah/050106.cha = = = [by other side the MOT means the black base around the pennies] " He just says socks, and he says long pants, but he doesn't mean it. I must get something for my baby bird to eat she said I will be back. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/07/5346GG.cha = = = "No!" is sometimes thought of as a toddler's favourite word, but often one of the most frequent words uttered by the parent of a toddler is "Don't!" It may seem that life with a young child is full of don'ts and other negative words: "Don't run out of the yard!" "Don't throw your toys!" "Don't hit!" Children need to have limits to keep them safe and to help them learn acceptable behaviours. Telling a child what not to do will enforce those limits for the moment, but it does not give him an understanding of what to do next time. Wherever possible, try to replace a "don't" with a "do". Focus on the behaviour you want, not the behaviour you want to avoid. Instead of "Don't touch the vase", try "Here is a toy for you to play with", and put the vase out of reach. Instead of "Don't hurt the kitty", say, "Let's pat the kitty gently like this". By giving a "do" instead of a "don't", you have shown your child what the rules are: the vase is not for playing and the kitty must be handled gently. You have given her an alternative, and she still has something to play with and is able to touch the kitty. As well, by using positive rather than negative words, you are more likely to get co-operation from your child. A child who is told "don't" may come back with a frustrated "no!" "Do" statements serve several purposes. They can be used to help your child understand the rules or limits and what is expected of him. Instead of "Don't leave your toys on the floor," saying, "Put the toys in the box when you are finished playing" tells him exactly what he needs to do. "Don't walk with your mouth full" could be "You need to sit at the table until you have eaten all your lunch." You can also use a "do" statement to redirect your child away from unwanted behaviour. These are the situations where we most likely want to say "don't!" because we want the child to stop what they are doing right now. "Don't rip the pages out of your book!" might be replaced by, "Here is some scrap paper for you to rip. I am going to put your book up so we can read it later." Instead of "Don't run in the store!" try "Walk with me and help me find the bananas." Children are learning and discovering new things every day, and they are bound to make mistakes. There are many situations where you need to say "don't" to your child, but when you can, try to use a "do" instead. By giving your child a positive alternative to a negative behaviour, you are helping her understand limits and form habits that will continue to develop as she grows. And hopefully, you will hear a few less "no!"s in response. Shawna Munro works at the Elspeth Reid Family Resource Centre, a facility of Child and Family Services of Western Manitoba that offers parenting information and support. 255 Ninth St., Brandon Republished from the Brandon Sun print edition October 25, 2012 Many houses in this town, producing a borough-interest, were included in the estate purchased by Mr. Venables, and the attorney with whom my brother lived, was appointed his agent, to collect and raise the rents. It is measured in kg/m3 or g/cm3 or g/mL. |Density ( ) = ||Mass| - Example of Density Rule Q. A stone has a mass of 10 grams. When it is immersed in a measuring cylinder of water, the water level rises 5 millilitres. What is the density of the stone? |A.||Density = ||Mass|| = ||10 grams|| = 2 g/mL| - Densities of Some Common Substances - Floating and Sinking The density of water is 1 g/mL. Any substance (such as ice, petrol or oil) whose density is less than 1 will float. Any substance (such as lead) whose density is more than 1 will sink. where are you going I'm going to put the baby down okay? I'm afraid it would get lost there. This wiki-style guide will provide you with various activities and ideas that cater to different age groups and preferences. The steps are designed to encourage positive interactions while ensuring safety and inclusivity. It ensures both parties have a vested interest in the chosen pastime, leading to more engagement and enthusiasm. *Key Tips & Guidelines:* + Start by listing potential interests such as sports, arts, board games, video games, movies, cooking, or outdoor activities. + Encourage open communication about personal favorites and dislikes. + Be considerate of any physical limitations or abilities. ### Step 2: Organize Age-Appropriate Activities **Explanation:** Selecting age-appropriate activities guarantees everyone involved has an equal opportunity to participate and contribute positively. *Key Tips & Guidelines:* + For younger children, opt for simple yet engaging tasks like coloring books, playdough sculptures, or building blocks. Soon, the bird was able to fly on its own. The bird was very happy and said, "Thank you, Lily, for being so kind and helping me." From that day on, Lily and the bird became good friends. They played together every day. The people in the street saw how Lily helped the bird and learned that being kind to others can make everyone happy. Edit Your Privacy Settings on YouTube Editing your privacy settings on YouTube is an important task that allows you to control who can see and interact with the content you upload, as well as protect your personal information. This tutorial will provide a detailed guide on how to edit these settings. 1. Sign in to your YouTube account: To begin editing your privacy settings, first sign in to your YouTube account using your Google credentials (email address and password). This is necessary because only registered users have access to their own privacy options. 2. Access your account settings: Once logged in, click on your profile picture at the top right corner of the page. A drop-down menu should appear; select 'Settings' from this menu. You will be redirected to the Account Overview page. 3. Navigate to Privacy: From the left sidebar, find and click 'Privacy'. Here, you will find several sections related to different aspects of your YouTube experience. We will go through them one by one. 4. Manage comments: The first section under Privacy is 'Comments'. It includes two main options - 'Allow all comments' or 'Hold potentially inappropriate comments for review'. Choosing the second option means that any comment marked as potential spam or containing offensive words will be hidden until you manually approve or reject it. This helps maintain a cleaner conversation space beneath your videos and reduces the risk of harassment. 5. Limit embedded video views: Next up is 'Embedded player restrictions', which lets you choose whether to allow embedding of your videos elsewhere on the web. Disabling this feature prevents others from embedding your videos on other websites, ensuring they can only be viewed directly on YouTube. Useful if you want to restrict where your content appears online. 6. Control likes & subscriptions visibility: Further down, there are separate toggles for controlling the visibility of liked videos and subscriptions. If set to private, neither will show up publicly on your channel page. However, note that unsubscribing from channels does not affect recommended videos based on watch history. 7. Adjust search preferences: Under 'Search', toggle off 'Include results from my liked videos and saved playlists' to exclude these from showing up when someone else uses the YouTube search bar while signed into your account. 8. Restrict location sharing: Towards the bottom of the page, disable 'Location Sharing' if you don't want YouTube knowing or storing your geographical location data. Remember though, some features might require location services to function correctly. 9. Save changes: After adjusting all desired settings, remember to hit 'Save' at the bottom of the page to ensure your new privacy choices take effect. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Regularly review your privacy settings as updates may change default behaviors or introduce new options. - Consider enabling Two Factor Authentication (2FA) for added security. Even if someone knows your password, they won't be able to log into your account without also having access to your phone or email linked to 2FA. - Be cautious about what personal details you share in video descriptions or comments. Any shared info could potentially be misused. By following this comprehensive guide and keeping our key tips in mind, you now know how to effectively manage your YouTube privacy settings! How does photosynthesis work? - Leaves of plants absorb light from the sun. - Leaves of plants also absorb the air that people breathe out, - Leaves of plants also absorb water. In some cases, two minerals have the same chemical composition but different crystal structures. For example, both diamond and graphite consist of just one element-carbon. But the arrangements of the carbon atoms in these two minerals are not the same, so they have different crystal structures and very different properties. Diamonds are extremely hard and have a brilliant sparkle. Graphite is soft, gray, and dull. Title: Entertain Your Pet Rock Introduction: While pets like dogs and cats are popular choices for companionship, some people prefer the simplicity and low maintenance of a pet rock. Although rocks don't have the same level of interaction as living animals, there are still ways you can entertain your pet rock and make its existence more engaging. Here is an extensive guide on entertaining your pet rock. Step 1: Choose the Right Rock Selecting the right rock is essential for entertainment purposes. A smooth, small-to-medium sized rock with an interesting shape would be ideal. This will allow for easier handling and provide visual stimulation when decorated or moved around. Key tip: Make sure the rock is clean before bringing it home, to avoid any potential health hazards. Step 2: Create a Habitat Create a dedicated space for your pet rock where it can rest and interact with its surroundings. You could place it inside a fishbowl or a decorative container that matches your home d cor. Add some substrate material such as sand, gravel, or colored pebbles to create a visually appealing environment. Guideline: The habitat should be big enough to accommodate the size of your rock but also compact enough to facilitate easy movement during playtime. Step 3: Decorate the Habitat Decorating the habitat adds to the aesthetic appeal and provides additional sources of interest for your pet rock. Consider adding items such as crystals, gemstones, or other natural elements found outdoors (e.g., pinecones). These objects offer different shapes, colors, and textures that may intrigue your pet rock. Remember always to sterilize these items before introducing them into the habitat to prevent bacterial growth. Tip: Rotate the decorations periodically to keep the environment fresh and exciting. Step 4: Introduce Light and Sound Rocks do not perceive light and sound like humans or animals; however, changing lighting conditions and sounds might affect their appearance and our perception of them. Place the habitat near windows with varying sunlight exposure throughout the day or introduce artificial lights with dimming capabilities. Additionally, playing soft background music or nature sounds could add another layer of sensory experience for both you and your pet rock. Keep in mind that loud noises should be avoided as they may cause unnecessary vibrations that could potentially harm the rock. Step 5: Engage Through Movement Although immobile themselves, rocks respond to external forces by altering their position or orientation. Regularly repositioning your pet rock within its habitat encourages exploration and discovery. Gently roll the rock along various surfaces, mimicking the effects of wind or water erosion. Be cautious not to scratch or damage delicate features on the rock's surface while moving it. Step 6: Observe Interactions Observation plays a crucial role in understanding your pet rock's behavior and preferences. Spend time watching how it interacts with its environment and take note of any patterns or reactions to changes made in the habitat. Over time, this observation will help inform future decisions regarding decorations, lighting, and activities tailored specifically to your pet rock's unique characteristics. Conclusion: While owning a pet rock does not involve traditional forms of engagement seen in live pets, there are still creative ways to enrich its existence and bring enjoyment to yourself through observation and appreciation. By following this comprehensive guide, you can ensure a fulfilling life for your pet rock and strengthen the bond between human and geological companion. Now he wasn't hungry anymore and he wasn't a little caterpillar anymore. Ella d' you want to go for a walk? How do bees get the nectar out of the flower? The mouthparts of a honey bee are made up of mandibles and a proboscis, or tongue. When the worker visits a flower, she brings forward the proboscis which is normally tucked under her "chin" and inserts it into the part of the flower where the nectar is. When she locates nectar, the bee sucks until she has extracted all that is within her reach. The nectar is stored in a honey sac, a second stomach, until she returns to the hive. The nectar load is transferred to worker bees in the hive who suck the nectar from the honey sac through their proboscis. They work the nectar, adding enzymes, until it is suitable for storing in a cell of the honeycomb. Sources: Winston, Mark L. The Biology of the Honey Bee.1987 Sammarato, Diana and Alphonse Avitabile. The Beekeeper's Handbook, 3rd ed. 1998 Graham, Joe M., ed. The Hive and the Honey Bee. 1992. What does the baby have around her? [Third home visit; elicited report took place in the living room, with MOT sitting on couch next to CHI] Challenging Perimeter Problem Perimeter of rectangle covered by 9 non-overlapping squares. From 200) American Invitational Math Exam Challenging Perimeter Problem Use this menu to view and help create subtitles for this video in many different languages. You'll probably want to hide YouTube's captions if using these subtitles. - Here's an interesting problem involving perimeter from the 2000 American Invitational Mathematics Exam. - It says, "The diagram shows a rectangle that has been dissected into 9 overlapping squares." One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine overlapping squares. - "Given that the width and the height of the rectangle are positive integers with greatest common divisor one...". - So, they're talking about the width and the height of the rectangle - The reason why they're saying "...the greatest common divisor one..." is... - ... they're saying that they don't have a common divisor that you can divide them both by to get a more simplified ratio. - And to think about that is we might be faced with two choices. One where maybe this side over here is... let me draw it like this... - ... 5 and 15, but over here our greatest common divisor isn't 1. They're both divisible by 5 over here. - So, what you'd want do is say... no, instead of 5 and 15, they need to be 1 and 3. - Now you have the same ratio of sides, but now the greatest common divisor is 1. - You have it in a simplified form, or the most simplified form if you divide both this height and this width by 5. - So, that's why they're saying "...the greatest common divisor of one..." - And then they say, "Find the perimeter of the rectangle." So, let's see what we can do here. - I encourage you to pause this and try to do it on your own before I bumble my way through this problem. - So, let's start at the beginning. Let's start with this square right over here, this center square. They did tell us that they're all squares. - So let's say that the square right over here has a length (X) and a height (X). It's an (X by X) square. Let me write it so this is an (X) and that is an (X). - So, this is an (X by X) square right over there. And then you have this square right over here. And we don't know its measurements. - So let's say that this square right over here is (Y by Y). So it has (Y) width and it also has (Y) height. - Now, what is this square over here? Well, this is an (X +Y) by (X + Y) square because the width of these two squares combined made the width of this larger square. - So I'm going to... actually, this might be an easier way to write it... since they're all squares, I'm going to write the dimension of that square inside the square. - So this is going to be an (X by X) square, kind of a non-conventional notation, but it will help us keep things a little bit neat. - This is going to be a (Y by Y) square, so I'm not saying the area is (Y), I'm saying it's (Y by Y). - This over here... (X + Y) is going to be each of its dimensions. It's going to be (X + Y) height and (X + Y) width. - Then this one over here... well, if this dimension is (X + Y) and this dimension right over here is (X)... - ... , then this whole side... or any of the sides of this square... is going to be the sum of that. - So, (X) + (X + Y) is (2X + Y). You can imagine that I'm just labeling the left side of each of these squares. - The left side of this square has length (Y), the left side of this one is (X), this one is (X + Y), this one is (2X + Y). - Then we can go to this one up here. Well, if this distance right over here is (2X + Y) and this distance right over here is (X + Y)... - ... you add them together to get the entire dimension of one side of this square. - So it's going to be (3X + 2Y). I just added the (2X) plus the (X) and the (Y) plus the (Y) to get (3X + 2Y)... - ... is the length of one dimension or one side of this square, and they're all the same. - Now let's go to this next square. Well, this length is (3X + 2Y) and this length is (2X + Y), then this entire length right over here is going to be (5X + 3Y). - (5X + 3Y) is going to be that entire length right over there. - We can also go to this side right over here where we have this length... I'll use the same color... this length is (3X + 2Y), this is (X + Y), and this is (Y). - So if you add (3X + 2Y) plus (X + Y) plus (Y), you get (4X + 4Y). And we can express this character's dimensions in terms of X and Y. - This is going to be (5X + 3Y)... and then you're going to have (2X + Y)... and then you're going to have (X). - So, you add the X's together. 5X + 2X is 7X... plus X is 8X. - And then you add the Y's together. 3Y + Y... and you don't have a Y there... so that's going to be + 4Y. That's the dimensions of this square. - And finally we have this square right over here. It's dimensions are going to be Y + 4X + 4Y, so that's 4X + 5Y. - And then if we think about the dimensions of this actual rectangle over here... if we think about its height right over there,.. - ... that's going to be 5X + 3Y + 8X + 4Y. So, 5X + 8X is 13X plus 3Y + 4Y is 7Y. - So, that's its height. We can also think about its height by going on the other side of it. Maybe this will give us some useful constraints. - This is going to have to be the same length as this over here. And so if we add 4X + 4X we get 8X. And then if we add 4Y + 5Y we get 9Y. - So these are going to have to be equal to each other, so that's an interesting constraint. - So we have 13X + 7Y is going to have to equal 8X + 9Y. - We can simplify this if you subtract 8X from both sides, you get 5X. - And if you subtract 7Y from both sides you get 5X = 2Y. Or you could say X = 2/5Y. - In order for these to show up as integers, we have to pick integers here, but let's see if we have any other interesting constraints... - ... if we look at the bottom and the top of this... if this gives us any more information. - So, if we add 5X + 3Y + 3X + 2 Y + 4X + 4Y... this top dimension... 5X + 3X is 8X + 4X is 12X. - And then you get 3Y + 2Y + 4Y is 9Y. That's this top dimension. - And if you go down here, you have 8X + 4X is 12X... let me do that in the same color... - ... and then you have 4Y + 5Y is 9Y. - So, these actually ended up to be the same in terms of X and Y, so they're not giving us any more information... no more constraints. - Obviously, 12X + 9Y is going to be equal to 12X + 9Y. - So, our only constraint on this problem is what we got by setting this left hand side equal to this right hand side... X needs to be equal to 2/5Y. - So, let's just pick some numbers so we get nice integers for X and Y, and then we can figure out the perimeter. - We want to make sure that the dimensions don't have any common divisors. - So if we pick Y to be equal to 5, then looking at this constraint, what is X? Well then, X is 2. It's going to be 2/5 times 5. So then X is equal to 2. - So, let's see what we get for the dimensions of this rectangle then. Her brethren also waxed wearie of this carriage in her; and having very often reproved her for it, without any other alteration in her: at length, they closely stole away the potte of Basile from her, for which she made infinite wofull lamentations, earnestly entreating to have it restored againe, avouching that shee could not live without it. Perceiving that she could not have the pot againe, she fell into an extreame sicknesse, occasioned onely by her ceaselesse weeping: and never urged she to have any thing, but the restoring of her Basile pot. Title: How to Fix an iPod That's Stuck in a Boot Loop An iPod stuck in a boot loop can be frustrating, but there are several methods you can try to fix this issue at home before seeking professional help. This tutorial will guide you through troubleshooting your device using clear steps with detailed explanations so that you understand what is happening every step of the way. ### Method 1: Force Restart Your iPod A force restart may solve simple software glitches causing the boot loop problem. The method varies depending upon which model of iPod you own. #### For iPod Touch (7th Generation): 1. Press and hold the Top button and the Volume up button until the Slide to Power Off screen appears. 2. Release both buttons then drag the slider from left to right to turn off your device. 3. Wait for about 30 seconds, press and hold the Top button again until the Apple logo shows up. **Explanation**: A forced restart does not erase any data; instead, it closes all running applications and restarts the system, eliminating minor bugs or issues leading to the boot loop problem. #### For Other Models (iPod Touch 6th Gen & Earlier, iPod Nano, Shuffle, Classic): 1. Press and hold the Sleep/Wake button and Home button simultaneously. 2. Continue holding these two buttons even if the screen turns off - keep holding them until you see the Apple Logo reappear. **Explanation**: Similar to the previous explanation, this action forces the device to shut down completely and start fresh, potentially removing whatever temporary problems were occurring during startup. ### Method 2: Charge Your iPod Battery If your iPod doesn't have enough power, it might get stuck in a boot loop. Connect your iPod to a charger and let it charge fully before attempting to turn it on again. **Key Tip**: Use only certified Apple charging cables and adapters to avoid damaging your device further. ### Method 3: Update/Restore via iTunes If neither of the above solutions worked, you could attempt updating or restoring your iPod via iTunes. Please note that restoring wipes out all user data, while updating keeps existing information intact. Therefore, opt for update first unless you recently backed up your data. 1. Install the latest version of iTunes on your computer. 2. Connect your iPod to the computer using its USB cable. 3. Launch iTunes, select your iPod when it appears in iTunes. 4. Click 'Check for Update' under the Summary tab to look for available updates. If one exists, click 'Download and Update'. Follow the prompts to complete the process. 5. If no updates are found or updating didn't resolve the issue, click 'Restore iPhone...' instead. Confirm the operation and allow iTunes to download and install the necessary files. Remember, this will delete everything on your iPod. **Guideline**: Always backup important data stored on your iPod either through iCloud or manually syncing with iTunes before performing an update or restore. ### Method 4: Contact Apple Support Persistent boot loops after trying the above steps indicate more serious hardware faults requiring expert attention. Reach out to Apple Support or visit an authorized service provider for assistance. They might need to replace certain components like batteries or logic boards. Remember, patience is crucial throughout this procedure as some techniques take time to execute correctly. And always ensure safety by never opening your iPod yourself unless appropriately trained to do so. Is there a hole in the ball? Look what you can do with it. The intensity of sunlight on Uranus is about 1/400 that on Earth. Its orbital elements were first calculated in 1783 by Pierre-Simon Laplace. With time, discrepancies began to appear between the predicted and observed orbits, and in 1841, John Couch Adams first proposed that the differences might be due to the gravitational tug of an unseen planet. In 1845, Urbain Le Verrier began his own independent research into Uranus's orbit. There is no clear evidence that treating schizophrenia with anti-psychotic drugs early is effective. The British NICE guidelines recommend cognitive behavioural talk therapy for all people at risk. In recent research, 76 patients at risk for schizophrenia were divided into two groups. One group got omega3 for 3 months and the other got a dummy pill (food oil). After 12 months only 4.9% of the omega 3 group had got schizophrenia compared to 27.5% in the other group. There is some evidence which shows that early treatment with drugs improves short term outcomes for people who have a serious episode of mental illness. These measures show little benefit five years later. Attempting to prevent schizophrenia in the pre-onset phase with anti-psychotic drugs is of uncertain benefit and so is not recommended (as of 2009). Prevention is difficult because there is no reliable way to find out in advance who will get schizophrenia. [hard to hear over the water running [3.19]] Title: How to Add Videos to a Blog from YouTube Introduction: Adding videos to your blog can greatly enhance its engagement, providing visual interest and breaking up text-heavy content. One popular source of videos is YouTube, which offers an easy way to embed videos into your blog posts. This tutorial will guide you through the process, with clear steps and helpful advice along the way. Step 1: Find a Suitable Video on YouTube Search for relevant content that aligns with your blog's theme or topic. Consider factors such as length, production quality, view count, and user comments when selecting a video. Look for official channels related to your niche, as these often provide reliable sources. By using appropriate videos, you create value for your audience while respecting copyright laws. Key Tip: Always preview a video before embedding it, ensuring there are no objectionable elements that could harm your reputation or offend visitors. Step 2: Copy the Video URL or Embed Code Once you have chosen a suitable video, copy its URL (web address) from the browser's address bar. Alternatively, click the 'Share' button below the video player and then select 'Embed'. A box containing HTML code will appear; copy this if you want more control over the embedded player settings. Guideline: If you choose to copy the HTML embed code, ensure your blog platform supports customization options like adjusting the size or disabling suggested videos at the end. Step 3: Log Into Your Blogging Platform Access your blogging account by entering your credentials. Navigate to the post editor where you wish to insert the video. Depending on the platform, you may be working with either a visual or text-based interface. Step 4: Insert the Copied Link or Embed Code In the post editor, place your cursor at the point where you would like the video to appear. Paste the copied link or embed code directly into the editing area. Most platforms automatically recognize YouTube links and convert them into embedded players. However, if manual formatting is required, refer to your platform's documentation for guidance. Step 5: Preview and Adjust Settings (Optional) Before publishing your post, preview it to check whether the video displays correctly. You might need to make minor adjustments, such as resizing the frame or tweaking alignment issues. Some platforms allow further customizations, so review available options to optimize the viewing experience. Key Tips: - To center the video, add <center></center> tags around the embedded code. - For responsive design, wrap the code within a div container and set width to 100%. Example: <div style="width:100%"><iframe src=""...></iframe></div> Step 6: Publish or Save Draft After verifying everything looks good, publish your updated blog post, making sure the video appears clearly for all viewers. If unsure about any aspect of the process, save your work as a draft and seek additional help - perhaps consulting online forums specific to your blogging platform. Conclusion: By following these steps, you now know how to add engaging YouTube videos to your blog posts. Not only does this enrich visitor experiences, but also positions you as a valuable resource within your niche. Happy blogging! Do you have books like that at home? Title: How to Download Free Beats on MusicBeats.net MusicBeats.net is an online platform that offers high-quality free beats for musicians, producers, and content creators. This step-by-step guide will walk you through the process of downloading free beats from the website while providing helpful tips and guidelines along the way. **Step 1:** Visit <https://www.musicbeats.net/> Open your web browser and navigate to MusicBeats.net's official website. By doing so, you gain access to their extensive library of free and premium beats. **Key Tip:** Bookmark the page for easy access in the future. **Step 2:** Browse the Categories On the homepage, explore various categories such as Hip Hop, R&B, Pop, Trap, and more. Click on any category that interests you to view available beats. **Guideline:** Take time to familiarize yourself with different genres and styles; this may help discover new inspirations or sounds for your projects. **Step 3:** Preview Beats Hover over a beat thumbnail to reveal the play button. Click it to listen to a preview of the track. Pay attention to its tempo, melody, and overall vibe to determine if it suits your needs. **Key Tip:** Use the built-in player's volume control to adjust the listening level according to your environment. **Step 4:** Select a Beat to Download Once you find a suitable beat, click on its title or image to proceed to its dedicated page. Here, you can learn more about the producer, license details, and other related information. **Step 5:** Choose the 'Free Download' Option Look for the 'Free Download' button, usually located near the bottom of the page. Click on it to initiate the download process. A pop-up window might appear asking for confirmation; confirm the action if necessary. **Guideline:** Always read the licensing agreement before downloading a free beat. Understand what rights and restrictions come with using the beat in your project. **Step 6:** Extract Downloaded File After clicking 'Free Download', your operating system should automatically save the file (usually in ZIP format) to your preferred 'Downloads' folder. Locate the ZIP file, right-click on it, and select 'Extract All'. Follow the prompts to unzip the file. **Key Tip:** Make sure your computer has an archiving tool installed. Most modern operating systems have one preinstalled. **Step 7:** Access Your Newly Downloaded Beat Upon extraction, you'll see individual MP3 files containing the instrumental beats. You can now import them into your digital audio workstation (DAW), video editor, or multimedia presentation software. **Congratulations!** You have successfully downloaded a free beat from MusicBeats.net. Remember to respect copyright laws and give proper credit to the producer when required. Enjoy creating music with your newly acquired resources! Above ground, the trunk gives height to the leaf-bearing branches, competing with other plant species for sunlight. In all trees the shape of the branches improves the exposure of the leaves to sunlight. Branches start at the trunk, big and thick, and get progressively smaller the farther they grow from the trunk.
