[Chi didn't want MOT to put the other pieces onto the black base] Title: How to Have the Perfect Form While Shooting a Basketball Introduction: Shooting a basketball is both an art and a science. It requires proper technique, practice, and consistency to make that perfect shot. This tutorial will guide you through the steps of achieving the ideal shooting form, helping you become a more accurate and efficient scorer. Remember, repetition is key; the more you practice with correct form, the more instinctive it becomes. Step 1: Hand Positioning Start by placing your dominant hand on the ball's seams, forming a 'trigger' or 'peace sign' shape with your index finger and thumb. Your non-dominant hand should be gently placed on the side of the ball for balance and support. The non-dominant hand should not interfere with the release of the ball. * Correct hand positioning allows for better control over the ball during the shot and ensures a smooth release. Step 2: Stance and Balance Position yourself shoulder-width apart, facing the basket directly. Bend your knees slightly and have your feet pointed towards the target. Keep your body weight balanced between the balls and heels of your feet. * A solid base enables greater stability when taking the shot, increasing accuracy and power. Step 3: Ball Lift and Elbow Alignment Lift the ball up using your shooting arm while keeping your elbow aligned under the ball. Maintain this alignment throughout the entire shooting motion - from lift off to release. Avoid letting your elbow flare outwards as this can lead to inconsistent shots. * Proper elbow alignment contributes to consistent releases and improved arc on your shot. Step 4: Eyes on Target Maintain eye contact with the target (the front rim or backboard) until after releasing the ball. Never look at the ball while shooting. Focusing on the target trains your mind where you want the ball to go. * Fixating on the target improves concentration and aim, ultimately resulting in higher chances of making the basket. Step 5: Guiding the Ball into Release As you reach the peak of your jump, bring the ball slightly above your head. At this point, your shooting arm should be almost fully extended but still maintaining its alignment. Beginners may find it helpful to imagine guiding the ball into a straight line before releasing it. * Bringing the ball above your head sets up the final stages of the shot. It also determines the height and trajectory of your shot. Step 6: Follow Through Snap your wrist forward upon releasing the ball. Extend your fingers toward the hoop even after the ball has left your hand. This whip-like action generates additional spin and speed which stabilizes the ball's flight path. * A good follow through enhances accuracy and teaches muscle memory for future shots. Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Practice regular dribbling drills to enhance ball handling skills and overall comfort level with the ball. 2. Consistency matters! Try to replicate the exact same motion every time you shoot. 3. Regularly stretch and warm up before practicing. Flexibility affects your range and ability to maintain form. 4. Film yourself shooting to identify any areas needing improvement. Visual feedback can accelerate progress. 5. Work on building strength in your core and legs. Stronger muscles translate to increased power behind your shots. 6. Always keep learning! Explore different techniques and adapt them according to what works best for you. Alright you lost interest in the book already, huh? Title: How to Call Internationally Introduction: International calling can seem like a daunting task if you've never done it before. However, with this comprehensive guide, we will walk you through every step necessary to make an international call from any phone. By following these steps carefully, you'll be able to connect with friends, family, or colleagues across the globe. Step 1: Determine the Country Code Every country has its unique country code that identifies it internationally. This is usually a two-to-four digit number preceded by a '+' sign. To find out the country code of the destination you want to call, visit websites such as CountryCode.org or search "[country name] country code" on your preferred search engine. Key Tip: Keep in mind some countries share the same country codes (e.g., Canada and the United States both have +1). Therefore, ensure you know which specific region within the shared country code you intend to reach. Step 2: Find Out the Local Area Code (Optional) If you are trying to reach a specific city or state within the foreign country, locate the local area code associated with that location. Similar to country codes, area codes also vary based on geographical regions. Again, consult resources like CountryCode.org or other online tools for accurate information. Step 3: Dial the Exit Code The exit code is what allows your call to leave the originating country. For North America, including the US and Canada, the exit code is 011; however, this may differ depending on where you are making the call from. Research the appropriate exit code for your location if it isn't 011. Step 4: Combine the Elements Now that you have obtained the country code, local area code (if applicable), and exit code, combine them sequentially without spaces or pauses. The sequence should look something like this (+)[exit code][country code][local area code(optional)]. Remember to replace bracketed items with their actual values. Example: If you were calling France from the U.S., you would dial 011-33 followed by the local area code and then the recipient's phone number. Step 5: Dial the Recipient's Phone Number After combining all previous elements, proceed to dial the intended recipient's full telephone number. Be sure to omit any initial zeros present in the number if they exist solely to denote the local area code. Step 6: Initiate the Call Once everything is entered correctly, press the call button on your device to establish connection. Wait patiently while the line rings until either voicemail picks up or someone answers. Additional Guidelines: * Long-distance calls using landlines tend to cost more than mobile carrier plans offering international minutes or VoIP services like Skype. Compare prices among different service providers to secure the best deal. * Some cellular carriers offer add-on packages specifically designed for frequent international communication. Consider purchasing one if it suits your needs. * Always double-check numbers before initiating a call to avoid misdials and unnecessary charges. * When receiving international calls, advise callers to exclude the leading zero from the exit code (e.g., instead of dialing 011, simply ask them to dial "+"). This simplification prevents confusion regarding proper entry procedure. * Lastly, familiarize yourself with time zone differences between your location and the destination to prevent inconveniencing recipients during late hours. In this type of abortion, a doctor gives the woman one or two medications that will end her pregnancy. A medication abortion can only be done early on in the pregnancy. This is because the medications that are used work best when they are started as early as possible, and after a woman has been pregnant for about two months, the medications usually do not work very well. Because of this, medication abortion usually is not used after a woman has been pregnant for 9 weeks. Some of the benefits (or reasons why some women choose this type of abortion) are that it can be started as soon as a woman realizes she is pregnant; it does not require anesthesia; and the woman does not have to have a procedure in a hospital or clinic to have the fetus removed, like with the other kind of abortion that is done. After a woman is given the medication or medications that end her pregnancy, the abortion happens like a "spontaneous" abortion or a miscarriage. Cleaning rollerblade bearings is an essential task that ensures optimal performance and longevity of your inline skates. Over time, dirt, debris, and moisture can accumulate in the bearings, causing them to become less efficient and eventually wear out. By regularly cleaning and maintaining your bearings, you'll enjoy smoother rides, better control, and save money by prolonging their lifespan. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of cleaning rollerblade bearings with precision, detail, and clarity. ### **Things You'll Need:** 1. A set of dirty rollerblade bearings 2. Citrus-based solvent or rubbing alcohol (isopropyl) 3. Two containers (one for cleaning solution, one for holding bearings) 4. Paper towels or clean microfiber cloths 5. Q-tips or small brushes (toothbrush works great) 6. Light machine oil or bearing lubricant (e.g., Bones Speed Cream, Tri-Flow Superior Lubricant) 7. Tweezers or pliers (optional - for removing shields) 8. Gloves (recommended - to protect hands from chemicals) 9. Workspace with good ventilation ### **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** Before starting the cleaning process, make sure you have all necessary materials at hand. Having everything ready will streamline the operation and prevent interruptions. Proper organization also reduces the risk of losing small parts like ball bearings or shields during the procedure. ### **Step 2: Remove the Wheels From the Frame** To access the bearings, first remove the wheels from the frame. Most frames feature removable spacers between the bearings; slide these off along with the wheel. Set aside the spacers, as they don't require cleaning in this process. ### **Step 3: Pop Out the Bearings** Next, carefully pop out the bearings from both sides of the wheel using your fingers, a screwdriver, or a specialized tool designed for this purpose. Place the extracted bearings into a container to keep them organized and prevent loss. ![Rollerblade Wheel](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ad/Remove-Inline-Skate-Wheels-Step-2-Version-3.jpg/aid105235-v4- Are you my mother he asked the kitten? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/11/5777FC.cha = = = [puts a toy figure in the cup] King John's reign began with military defeats - he lost Normandy to Philip II of France in his first five years on the throne. The Chi-Square is a "Ratio Ruling" The ratios might be slightly "______" (a math term for "off") due to the number of individuals you collected or just "___ ____". P = smooth seeds crossed with wrinkled seeds F1 = all smooth seeds (so smooth is ________ and wrinkled is _________) F2 = 5,474 smooth seeds and 1,850 wrinkled seeds is a ratio of 2.96 : _ Mendel and the Punnett square tell us that we should have a ratio of _ : 1 not 2.96 : 1! It depends upon how close the ______, observed numbers are from the __________, expected numbers. Mendel used the chi-square (abbreviated 2) test. The chi-square test, or simply the "chi-square", ________ the significance of the data in comparison with what you expect to get. The 2 only requires that you know the ______ of individuals observed in each category and what numbers you expected them to be. In chi-square analysis you compare the number of individuals of a certain phenotype (or ________ else) that you have found in the experiment to the number you ________ to have. You find the difference between the observed and the expected by simply subtracting one from the other. Then, you square it (multiply it by ______). That gives you the "squared difference". Then you ______ the "squared difference" by what you expected in the first place in order to give you a "squared difference per expected" for that group. Take into account all the different _____ by adding together these "squared differences per expected" values for each class. The final sum (of the "squared differences per expected") gives you a number called the 2. F2 = 5,474 smooth seeds and 1,850 wrinkled seeds for a ratio of ____ : 1 We use the "___ _______" of the data and compare it to the "raw numbers" we expected. Step 1: calculate the ________ number of each type. Add together both seed types = a total population of _____ seeds. Of those 7,324 seeds you expected a _______ of them (1 in 4) to be wrinkled. So, how many of those 7,324 seeds should be wrinkled? How many smooth seeds should you expect from the total of 7,324 seeds? We expected _____ smooth seeds and _____ wrinkled seeds. Step 2: calculate the "squared differences per ________". We observed 5,474 smooth seeds but expected 5,493 a DIFFERENCE of __. Next we ______ THE DIFFERENCE. 19 x 19 (or 192) = 361. Next we find the SQUARE OF THE DIFFERENCE PER ________ by dividing that number by the total number of smooth seeds we expected to see (5,493). So that is 361/5,493 = _____ Now the next class. We observed 1,850 wrinkled seeds but expected _____. That's a difference of 19. Square the difference to get 361. Divide it by the expected number of ________ seeds (NOT the expected number of ______ seeds - a common mistake) so that is ___/_____ = 0.197 Step 3: congratulate yourself for having got through the ________ part! Step 4: ___ up the "squared differences per expected" from all the categories. 0.066 + 0.197 = _____ This experiment (above) has a chi-square equal to 0.263 (2 = 0.263). Step 5: _______ our chi-square value to the value in a chi-square significance table and determine if our value is significant. For our work we want to know if these results pass a significance level of __. Your degrees of freedom are one less than the number of __________ you have to work with. We have two categories, smooth and wrinkled, so we have one ______ of _______. With one degree of freedom we could be allowed a chi-square as large as ____ and the results would still be considered ___________ to 5%. We would have to get a chi-square value ____ 3.84 before we would say that our results were so far from a 3 : 1 ratio that we would have to ______ that ratio (and Mendel's explanation of how he got that ratio). With a 2 = 0.263 there's less than a 5% chance that this 3:1 ratio happened by ________. The Chi-square is a kind of "mathematical judge" of _____________. Imagine Mendel ________ in this experiment 5,493 smooth seeds and 1,831 wrinkled seeds. That is an exact 3 : 1 _____. Looking first at the smooth seeds we would see that the difference between the observed and expected is ____! When we square zero we still get zero. If we divide zero by the expected value we get ____! The same happens when we calculate the values for the wrinkled seeds too. Now we would add those two values together (because they are the "squared differences averaged") to get a final 2 = _. In other words, when the chi-square equals zero the experimental results are in exactly the ratio ________! Conversely, the _______ the chi-square gets from zero the less likely the ratio "rule" is being followed. We could have got a chi-square value as high as ____ and still feel that we were close enough to the 3 : 1 ratio to not be worried. Here's another set of results from Mendel's monohybrid cross experiments. Let's do the chi-square analysis of it. P = green seeds crossed with yellow seeds F1 = all yellow seeds (So which color is dominant?) F2 = 6,022 yellow seeds and 2,001 green seeds First, calculate the ________ number of each type. You have a total of 8,023 seeds (6,022 yellow + _____ green = 8,023). The green seeds should make up a quarter of that population, so dividing 8,203 by _ gives you 2,005.75. That means you ________ 2,005 and seeds. The yellow seeds should make up the rest of the sample so we can find their number by subtracting 2,005.75 from the total 8,023 to get ________. So, from the total of 8,023 seeds you expected 6,017.25 to be yellow and 2,005.75 to be green. Second, calculate the "_______ ___________ per ________". You expected 2,005.75 greens but ________ 2001 and that is a difference of 4.75 (2,005.75 - 2,001 = 4.75). Square that number you get _____. Divide it by the number you expected (________) you get 0.011. You expected 6,017.25 yellows but observed 6,022 and that is a difference of 4.75 (6,017.25 - 6,022 = -4.75). Square that number you get 22.56. Divide it by the number you expected you get (6,017.25) to get _____. Third, add up the "squared differences per expected" from ___ the categories. That's 0.011 + 0.004 = 0.015 so your 2 = _____. We are working with only ___ degree of freedom. The ratio observed in this experiment The chi-square formula is 2 = [(O - E)2/E] "O" is the number ________ and "E" is the number ________. The symbol "" (called "sigma") is used throughout math to mean "___". Let's assume the results of a cross were 3,087 yellow seeds and 2,937 green seeds. There are _____ seeds in total. If the 3 : 1 ratio applies then one quarter of them should be green. That means 1,506 should be green and the rest (4,518) should be ______. Doing the greens first, that's (O - E)2/E = (2,937 - 1,506)2/____ = 1359.7 The yellows will be (O - E)2/E = (_____ - _____)2/4,518 = 453.2. Now add them together (that's what S means) to get a 2 = ______. Our calculated chi-square shows that these experimental results are well _______ the acceptable range for a 3 : 1 ratio so we ______ the idea that these results represent a 3 : 1 ratio. What ratio are they close to and how would you test the ratio to see if it is close enough? Do another chi-square on that same data (3,087 yellow seeds and 2,937 green seeds) and see if it is close enough to a _ : _ ratio. There is a total of 6024 seeds so a 1: 1 ratio should show us 3,012 yellow seeds and _____ green seeds. The greens will be (O - E)2/E = (2,937 - 3,012)2/3,012 = _____. The yellows will be (O - E)2/E = (_____ - 3,012)2/3,012 = 1.867 (again). Adding them together gives me the 2 = 3.734 and compare that with the Table. The new 2 , using a 1 : 1 ratio, is low enough to be within the range of significance. (This 2 is less than 3.84.) Therefore, the results of this experiment are too far from being 3 : 1. I think they are ______ 1 : 1. The chi-square is used whenever you want to compare the observed results to the ones you would expect from a certain ____. Title: How to Keep Your Lips Smooth and Soft Introduction: Maintaining smooth and soft lips is essential for your overall appearance and comfort. Harsh weather conditions, chapped air, or even frequent talking can lead to dry, cracked, and uncomfortable lips. Fortunately, by following simple steps consistently, you can keep your lips looking their best. This article will provide an all-inclusive guide on how to maintain smooth and soft lips. Step 1: Exfoliate Regularly Exfoliation removes dead skin cells that accumulate on your lip's surface, revealing softer and smoother skin underneath. Use a gentle exfoliating product specifically designed for lips or create a homemade scrub using sugar and honey or olive oil. Gently massage the mixture onto your lips with circular motions before rinsing off with warm water. Key tip: Don't overdo it; limit exfoliation to once or twice a week to avoid irritation. Step 2: Stay Hydrated Drinking plenty of fluids ensures proper hydration levels within your body, which reflects directly on your skin, including your lips. Aim for at least eight glasses of water per day. If possible, opt for herbal teas, fresh fruit juices, or infused waters instead of caffeinated drinks, as they may dehydrate you further. Guideline: Monitor your urine color throughout the day - if it's light yellow or clear, you are well-hydrated. Step 3: Protect from Sun Damage Just like other parts of your face, your lips need sun protection too. Apply a lip balm containing SPF 15 or higher whenever going outside during daylight hours. UV radiation causes premature aging, leading to wrinkles around the mouth area and increases the risk of skin cancer. Always reapply after eating, drinking, or every two hours when exposed to sunlight. Step 4: Moisturize Frequently Lip moisturizers help retain natural moisture while forming a protective barrier against external elements. Choose a lip balm enriched with ingredients such as shea butter, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E, or aloe vera. These components lock in hydration and promote healing for any existing damage. Remember also to apply lip balm before bedtime to allow deep nourishment overnight. Step 5: Avoid Harmful Habits Certain habits can contribute to dryness and discomfort of the lips. Refrain from licking your lips frequently, as saliva evaporates quickly, leaving them drier than before. Biting or peeling dried skin should also be avoided since this action can cause bleeding, infection, or more severe cracking. Lastly, quit smoking, as tobacco contains chemicals harmful to both oral health and general wellbeing. Step 6: Maintain Good Oral Hygiene Proper dental care plays a vital role in maintaining healthy lips. Brush your teeth twice daily, floss regularly, and consider using a tongue scraper to remove bacteria buildup. Poor oral hygiene might result in inflammation, bad breath, or gum diseases affecting your lip's condition negatively. Additionally, visit your dentist routinely for checkups and cleanings. Conclusion: By incorporating these practices into your daily routine, you'll notice significant improvements in your lip's texture and overall appearance. Consistency is crucial; therefore, make sure to allocate time for lip care just as you would for skincare or haircare regimes. Soon enough, others will start complimenting you on your smooth and soft lips! what kind of money is this? And I could tell you jokes okay said Kermit as they climbed into the boat. Keys yeah you got the keys alright. king&i wrote:The roof of a sports arena is supported by hyperbolic arches anchored to the ground. These arches span a distance of sixty meters and have a maximum height of 20 meters. a) Find an equation for a hyperbola to model one of these arches. Draw a picture of this situation. Well, okay; draw just the lower "half" of the hyperbola, representing the roof's arch, and the x-axis, representing the ground. Since the arches are 60 units wide, then, if you (for simplicity's sake) center the drawing on the y-axis, the x-intercepts will be at x = -30 and x = +30. Label your picture with this information. Since the peak is 20 units high, then the vertex (of the one half) is at y = 20. Label your picture with this information. The center will obviously be on the y-axis, so (h, k) = (0, k). Since the vertex is "a" units from the center, then k = 20 + a. Label your picture with this information. Note that this relationship means that a = k - 20. The conics equation of an hyperbola of this form is:. . . . . Since you already know that h = 0, then:. . . . . You have three points on the hyperbola: (-30, 0), (0, 20), and (30, 0). Since the first and third will work out pretty much the same, plug the second and the third into the above equation, and get equations with unknowns "a", "b", and "k". But a = k - 20, so:. . . . .. . . . . For this second equation, note that 20 - k = -(k - 20), and the squaring takes care of the "minus" sign, so you really have:. . . . . ...which is true, but pretty pointless. This tells you that the reason you've been asked to find "an" equation, rather than "the" equation, is that there may be more than one equation possible! So work with what you've got, and pick something for "k". Then this will give you a value for "a", and then you can solve for the value of "b". king&i wrote:b) What is the height of the arch at a horizontal distance of 25 meters from the ceiling's peak? Twenty-five units from the peak is twenty-five units to the right (or the left) of the y-axis. So plug "x = 25" into your equation, and see what you get. What we did was we went to Nana's and back. I know- how about another post about spelling- back to back! I wanted to share one more activity that Moonpie and I did today at the last minute. She was struggling to come up with more than two -ake words, so we made a game out of it. I printed out a template used for geometry instruction. It is a cube you can cut out and tape together. On one of the cubes I wrote -ake on every side. On the other cube I wrote different beginning sounds, like 'l', 'm', 'b', etc. She would roll the cubes like dice and put them together to see what word she made. Then, she would write the word on her paper (clipped to her very official clipboard). It's a very easy way to make word work a little more engaging and fun. Here are some visuals: step 1: You must dress up like a kitty cat. This is crucial. Do not skip this step! Wait, that wasn't the first step. Roll the dice. Yes, that sounds more like it! step 2: put the dice together and see what word you have made. step 3: write your word on your very official paper clipped to your very official clipboard. I have made up another cube with new beginning sounds that we will use tomorrow to keep the list growing. If you are not a homeschooler, this is a fun way to have your child practice their spelling words, or just practice making words in general. Some variations would be to put a different word ending on each side of one cube (at, it, ock, etc.) and different starting sounds on the other. Keep in mind that your child will probably come up with some very creative words. On their paper you can have a column for non-sense words. Every word made should be written down. It will be good practice for you child to be able to differentiate between true words and non-sense words. Or you can even go nuts and write numbers on each side! Throw caution to the wind and use the cubes to practice math facts. Keeping something as versatile as this on hand is an easy way to make language and math work a bit more fun! Title: How to Be Different ========================= Step 1: Embrace Your Uniqueness ------------------------------- Start by accepting yourself for who you are, including all your quirks and differences. Understand that everyone is unique in their own way, and there's nothing wrong with standing out from the crowd. By embracing your individuality, you will automatically become different from those around you who may be trying too hard to fit in. Key Tips: * Focus on what makes you special and interesting instead of comparing yourself to others. * Celebrate your achievements, no matter how small they might seem compared to someone else's accomplishments. Step 2: Develop Your Interests and Passions ------------------------------------------ Pursue activities and hobbies that truly interest you, even if they aren't popular or mainstream. This could include anything from learning a new language, playing an unusual instrument, or participating in a niche sport. Dedicating time to these interests will help shape your personality and make you stand out as an expert in your field. Key Guidelines: * Don't worry about whether your hobby is considered "cool" or not; focus on enjoying the process of learning and growing. * Share your passion with others by joining clubs, attending events, or creating content related to your interest. Step 3: Cultivate Individual Style ---------------------------------- Express yourself through fashion choices that reflect your personality. Instead of following trends blindly, create a wardrobe that showcases your uniqueness. You can mix and match styles, colors, and patterns to develop a look that's truly one-of-a-kind. Key Tips: * Invest in quality pieces that represent your taste and last longer than fast fashion items. * Remember that confidence is the best accessory - wear clothes that make YOU feel good! Step 4: Think Critically and Question Norms ----------------------------------------- Don't be afraid to challenge conventional wisdom and question societal norms. By thinking critically about the world around you, you'll come up with fresh ideas and perspectives that set you apart from others. Key Guidelines: * Stay informed about current events and engage in thoughtful discussions with people who have differing opinions. * Always strive to learn more and expand your understanding of various topics. Step 5: Communicate Effectively ------------------------------ Develop strong communication skills to express your thoughts clearly and persuasively. Being able to articulate your ideas effectively will help you stand out in any situation, whether it's at work, school, or social gatherings. Key Tips: * Practice active listening to better understand others and build rapport. * Use storytelling techniques to make your points more engaging and memorable. Step 6: Build Genuine Relationships ------------------------------------ Focus on forming meaningful connections with people based on shared values and interests rather than superficial qualities. Authentic relationships built on trust and respect will naturally make you stand out as someone worth knowing. Key Guidelines: * Show genuine interest in getting to know others by asking open-ended questions and actively listening to their responses. * Offer support and encouragement when needed, fostering a sense of mutual growth and development. Remember, being different doesn't mean rejecting everything mainstream or trying to be completely unique in every aspect of your life. It's about finding balance between embracing your individuality and connecting with others. By following these steps, you'll cultivate a distinct identity that sets you apart while still allowing you to form meaningful connections with those around you. [picks up yellow wand which has several objects stuck to it] Title: How to Conserve Resources in Resident Evil 4 Resident Evil 4 is a classic survival horror game that requires players to manage their resources carefully in order to survive. This tutorial will provide you with detailed steps on how to conserve your resources in Resident Evil 4, ensuring you make it through the game with as few restarts as possible. **Step 1: Prioritize Healing Items** Firstly, prioritize using healing items only when necessary. Your health should always be your top priority. If you're low on health but expect to encounter more enemies soon, use a healing item before proceeding. However, if you're in an area where enemy encounters are unlikely, try to hold onto your healing items until they're needed more urgently. Key Tip: The herbs found throughout the game can be combined to create stronger healing items. Combine a green herb with a red one for moderate healing, or add another green herb to double the effect. Use this strategy wisely to maximize your healing potential. **Step 2: Manage Ammunition Efficiently** Ammunition management is crucial in Resident Evil 4. Avoid firing wildly into crowds of enemies; instead, aim for headshots or weak points to take them down quickly and save bullets. Also, consider which weapon types are most effective against different enemy types - shooting a chainsaw-wielding Ganado with a handgun might waste valuable ammo better saved for faster, smaller foes. Guideline: Regularly check your inventory and discard any weapons you no longer need or use frequently. Every weapon takes up space that could otherwise be used for precious ammo. **Step 3: Make Use of Environmental Objects** Explosive barrels, gas cans, and other environmental objects can help conserve ammo by providing alternative ways to dispatch enemies. Look out for these opportunities during combat situations. Shooting an explosive barrel near several enemies, for example, can take out multiple targets at once without wasting your own bullets. Key Tip: Be careful when destroying these objects, though, as doing so may also eliminate any nearby pickups like ammo or treasure! **Step 4: Save Often But Strategically** Saving your progress often is important, especially since dying means losing all the items you were carrying (except those stored in the case). But remember, every time you save, you consume a typewriter ribbon. Try to limit saves to times when you've collected new weapons, treasures, or made significant progress in the story. Guideline: Before saving, ensure you have cleared the immediate area of enemies to prevent ambushes while you're vulnerable at the typewriter. **Step 5: Engage Enemies Selectively** Not every enemy needs to be fought. Some can simply be avoided by sneaking past them or taking detours around them. Reserve engaging in battles for moments when it's unavoidable or beneficial for gathering resources. Key Tip: Listen for audio cues indicating approaching enemies so you know when to prepare for battle or move quietly to avoid detection. By following these steps, you'll find yourself conserving resources effectively in Resident Evil 4, making the challenging journey just a little bit easier. Remember, resource conservation isn't about hoarding; it's about using what you have smartly and efficiently. Good luck! No you have to put 'em together like this. By Photocopies of the The Three Billy Goats Gruff Reader's Theater script for each student (I used a version found in Reader's Theater, Grade 1 by Evan-Moor published in 2003.) Dry erase board with marker/ overhead/ Smart Board presentation with sentences: "We are having hot dogs for lunch" and 'The red team won the ball game". Evaluation sheet for each student (below) Previous student exposure to the book The Three Billy Goats Gruff (version of the teacher's preference) would be an optional, but favorable, condition prior to using this lesson plan. Procedures for carrying out the lesson in detail, with numbered steps. 1) Explain the importance of reading with expression and model the skill. "The better we learn to read, the more reading out loud will begin to sound the same as when we talk. When we talk, you can hear in our voices and tell by our faces if we are sad, happy, excited, or angry. We call that expression. I am going to read the same sentence to you two different ways. See if you can tell the difference in my expression. If I came up to you and said ( revealing sentence and speaking as if upset), 'We are having hot dogs for lunch today' would you think that I liked hot dogs? Probably not. I sounded a little mad that we were having hot dogs because my voice was lower and my face was a little pouty. But if I came up to you and said (excitedly) 'We are having hot dogs for lunch today!' would you think that I liked hot dogs? It sounds like I do! I sounded happy about having hot dogs! I said that with a happy expression in my voice and a happy look on my face. My voice sounded high and cheerful. 2) Practice reading some sentences as a class. "Let's see if you can try reading with expression. Everyone read this sentence together. (reveal: The red team won the ball game.) Great! Now let's read it again, and this time, let's read it like we are all fans of the red team, and we are really excited that they won the game! Great job! I heard lots of high, happy voices and saw some happy faces! Now, let's read it again, but this time, let's pretend like we are fans of the blue team, and we are really sad that the red team won. Wonderful! I heard lots of lower voice tones, and saw some sad looks on some faces! 3) Introduce reader's theater. "When we go to a play, or watch a movie, the actors and actresses use expression in their voice to help tell the story and make it seem real. Last week, we read a story called The Three Billy Goats Gruff. Today, I have the scripts for a reader's theater for that same story! I am going to hand out the scripts to each reading group, and you will be the highlighted character on the script that I hand you. In this story, there is a little billy goat, a medium billy goat, and a big billy goat. (Talking in a little voice) I think the little billy goat might talk like this! What do you think? Can everyone say, 'I am the little billy goat with me in their best little billy goat voice? Ready, go! I am the little billy goat! Excellent! I heard some wee little voices! What do you think the big billy goat might sound like? Wonderful! I heard some biiig, deep voices. What about the mean old troll who says, 'You can't cross my bridge'? Yeah, he sounds a little angry and grumpy! 4) Pass out scripts so that each reading group gets every character in the story. "First, I want each of your to whisper-read your parts to yourself to make sure you know all of the words in your part. Then, practice reading the scripts among your group and pretend you are getting ready for the upcoming school play! Remember to use lots of expression to help tell the story! I will walk around and give you help and listen for some great expressive readers." As you circulate the room, listen to students to see if they are using expression. You might help them with prompts, such as "How do you think he is feeling when he says that?" I have also found that modeling the same voice yourself while posing questions, such as saying (in a big mean voice), "How do you think that mean old troll sounds here?" really helped students to get into the spirit of the reading to see that you yourself are having fun with it. Listen to each groups performance, and answer the following questions about each child: -Was the student able to use voice tone to portray expression? -Was the student able to use facial movement to portray expression? -Did the student's application of expression show comprehension of the mood of the story? It may help to carry a script around with you and write the student's initials on your copy next to an excerpt where you noticed them using expression well. Reference to a source that can tell us more. Provide all we need to track down the source. These alternate lesson plan ideas for expressive reading are available online: -Maggie Saye, 'Express Yourself?'. -Amy Berger, 'We're Messin' With Expression'. Sis you ready for some more fries? Hello young explorers! Today we're going to learn about something called "Data Grid Conversions." It's like when you take a picture of your toys arranged on your bedroom floor and turn it into a neat little chart that shows where each toy is located. Imagine you have a big collection of different kinds of data - maybe drawings, photographs, numbers, even weather information! Sometimes this data needs to be organized in a special way before you can compare it or combine it with other data. That's where Data Grid Conversions come in handy. Think of it like moving your toys around on the floor to fit them into boxes. Each box has its own space that fits just a few toys perfectly. In our case, these "boxes" are actually equally sized squares on a grid - imagine graph paper. We want to put all our data points (the toys) inside their very own square (box), no matter how many there are. This makes it easy to see patterns, compare things, or create beautiful pictures out of the data. Now suppose you took a photo of the toys after placing them carefully inside the boxes. But wait! You realized that the photo doesn't match the actual arrangement anymore because some toys may appear larger or smaller depending on where they were placed. To fix this problem, we will rearrange our toys using a magical tool called "Datashader." With Datashader, we can quickly resize the images of the toys (data points) so that they fit nicely back onto our graph paper again. Once everything lines up perfectly, we get a clearer understanding of how our toys relate to each other and the whole room! And that's basically what Data Grid Conversion does! By turning messy data into orderly grids, we open doors to endless possibilities for exploration and discovery. Next time you find yourself surrounded by piles of unorganized data, remember the power of Data Grid Conversions and bring order to chaos! Happy exploring! It is highly recommended that you focus solely on driving while operating a vehicle. Engaging in exercises or physical activities while driving can be extremely dangerous and increase the risk of accidents, injuries, and fatalities. Always prioritize safety above all else when behind the wheel. Introduction: While we strongly advise against performing actual exercises while driving due to the inherent dangers involved, there are some simple movements and stretches you can do during periods of prolonged sitting in your car. These gentle motions can help alleviate tension, reduce discomfort, improve circulation, and maintain overall well-being without compromising road safety. Title: How to Build Trust with Your Parents to Earn Your Stuff Back Introduction: Rebuilding trust with your parents after losing their confidence can be challenging but is definitely possible. It requires time, effort, consistency, and demonstrating maturity. This guide will walk you through steps to help regain your parent's trust so that you may earn back any privileges or belongings they have taken away. Remember, this process might take longer than expected, depending on the severity of the situation, but patience and perseverance are crucial. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Mistakes * Explanation: Admit what you did wrong and accept responsibility for your actions. By acknowledging your mistakes, you show self-awareness and emotional intelligence, which are essential building blocks for rebuilding trust. * Key tip: Be sincere and specific about what you did wrong, rather than making vague statements. Avoid blaming others or making excuses. Step 2: Apologize Sincerely * Explanation: Offer a genuine apology without expecting anything in return. Show empathy towards your parents by expressing understanding of how your behavior affected them negatively. * Guideline: Use an effective apology formula such as "I am sorry for _______. I understand that my action hurt/upset you because __________. I won't do it again." Step 3: Create a Plan for Change * Explanation: Develop a clear plan outlining the changes you intend to make moving forward. Identify triggers and temptations associated with past behaviors and develop strategies to overcome these challenges. * Key tip: Share this plan with your parents and seek their input. Collaborate on setting achievable goals and timelines together. Step 4: Demonstrate Accountability * Explanation: Prove your commitment to change by consistently following through on your promises and sticking to the agreed-upon plan. Hold yourself accountable for your decisions and actions. * Guideline: Regularly check in with your parents regarding your progress and discuss any setbacks openly and honestly. On Tuesday, he ate through two pears, but he was still hungry. Once there was a little boy named Ben. Ben liked shiny things, so when he saw a copper penny on the ground he ran over to get it. He liked the way the penny glinted in the sun. But when Ben picked the penny up, he noticed a little ant underneath. The ant was trying hard to carry a small piece of food back to its nest. Ben knew the penny was too heavy for the ant, so he decided to help. He knew the ant would not give up, so Ben insisted that he take the penny inside his house. He placed the penny in a special place in his room and promised to help the ant get its food back. He was determined and very persistent. After several tries Ben was able to get the food to the ant's nest. The ant was very happy and thanked Ben for his kindness. Ben smiled and thanked the ant back. He was glad the ant was safe, and now he could keep the shiny copper penny that he found. In everyday talk, we say things fall because the Earth's gravity pulls on them. We talk as if our weight was a "given". Actually, weight changes when the pull of gravity changes. The Moon is much smaller and the pull of gravity on the Moon is about 1/6th that of Earth. So any object on the Moon weighs 1/6th of its weight on Earth. What does not change is the amount of matter in an object. That is called conservation of mass. On Earth, mass and weight are the same for most purposes, though a sensitive gravimeter can detect the difference. The difference can be very different on another world such as the Moon. [places the plastic teddy bear on the black base in front of one of the silver men] 'Kay did she seem to like it? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV1/TP/jebtp1.cha = = = Document with formatting marks. Imagine that you have typed a few paragraphs. The paragraphs seem very far apart, and the second paragraph starts farther to the right than the first paragraph. You can see what's going on by looking at the formatting marks Word automatically inserts as you type. These marks are always in documents, but they are invisible until you display them. To see formatting marks, use the Ribbon, at the top of the window. On the Home tab, in the Paragraph group, click the Show/Hide button . Click the button again to hide formatting marks. These marks are not just for show. You can get rid of extra spacing by deleting extra marks. The first time you see these marks, you might wonder, "What's that?" And you might worry that formatting marks will be printed. These marks do not print - they won't be on printed pages, even when you see them on the screen. So what are formatting marks, and what do they mean? Here are a few examples: Word inserts a paragraph mark each time you press ENTER to start a new paragraph. In the picture, there's an extra paragraph mark between the two paragraphs, which means that ENTER was pressed twice. This creates extra space. Deleting the extra paragraph mark will get rid of the extra space between the paragraphs. One arrow appears each time TAB is pressed. In the picture there is one arrow in the first paragraph and two arrows in the second paragraph, so TAB was pressed twice in the second paragraph. Dots show how many times you press the SPACEBAR between each word, or if you accidentally press the SPACEBAR between letters in a word. One dot is one space; two dots are two spaces. Normally there should be one space between each word. Dots, by the way, are different from periods at the ends of sentences. Periods (which you always see) are on the bottom of the line. Dots are higher up, toward the middle of the line. In the practice you'll see how to get rid of extra space by deleting extra formatting marks. I Like to Shuffle Along in My Shiny Shoes Beginning Reading Lesson Plan Brittany Anne Hanie Rationale: In order to read and spell words it is important that children can identify letters and their phonemes. It has been shown that letter knowledge is one of the best predictors of reading success. This letter will introduce the letters s and h, the sound than corresponds with these letters when combined (sh), and the mouth movements for the combination of the two letters. Materials: Primary paper, pencil, chart with "Shelby and Shawn like to shuffle in their shiny shoes."; letterboxes for each student with small letters needed for the lesson; One Fish Two Fish Red Fish Blue Fish (by: Dr. Seuss, Random House Books, 1960); dry/erase board; marker 1. "Today we are going to talk about the letters s and h. We are going to practice these letters together. Who knows what sound s makes? What about h? When we put those two letters together it makes a sound that is a little bit different. This sound is ssshhhh. Can everybody practice making that sound? Very good." 2. "Remember how we sometimes use tongue twisters to help us remember sounds? Well, I have a new tongue twister for us to practice: Shelby and Shawn like to shuffle in their shiny shoes. Let's all practice saying this together. Very good. Now this time let's all try to stretch the /sh/ at the beginning of the words. SSShhelby and SSShhawn like to sshhuffle in their sshhiny sshhoes. That was wonderful. As we are going through our activities today, let's remember /sh/ by remembering the shiny shoes. 3. "We are now going to practice writing the letters s and h. On Earth, lightning makes plasma. Artificial (man-made) uses of plasma include fluorescent lightbulbs, neon signs, and plasma displays used for television or computer screens, as well as plasma lamps and globes which are a popular children's toy and room decoration. Scientists are experimenting with plasma to make a new kind of nuclear power, called fusion, which would be much better and safer than ordinary nuclear power, and would produce much less radioactive waste. The ancient Romans named the planet after their god Jupiter (Latin: "Iuppiter"). Jupiter is the third brightest object in the night sky. Only the Earth's moon and Venus are brighter. An insect's body consists of three main parts: the head, thorax and abdomen The vertex is the area above and between the eyes, the genae are the areas below and behind the eyes. The thorax consists of three segments, the prothorax, the mesothorax and the metathorax - each of which carries a pair of legs. Wings are carried on the first two or, if only one pair of wings, on the middle one. The abdomen has up to eleven segments. On the end of the abdomen their is often a pair of appendages called cerci. Some insects such as dragonflies use these to grasp the female while mating. Some insects have a long needle-like ovipositor at the end of the abdomen. This is used for laying eggs in wood or even in other live insects! Insects have one pair of compound eyes made up of lots of tiny lenses. Many insects also have very simple eyes called ocelli. There are usually three ocelli on the top or front of the head. All insects have one pair of antennae which are used to sense smell and touch All insects have three pairs of legs. You can usually see four main parts: The coxa, which joins the leg to the thorax, the femur, usually the largest segment. Next the tibia, usually the longest segment. Finally the tarsus or foot which consists of one to five segments with one or two claws at the end. Most insects have wings. They usually have two pairs of wings. In those such as butterflies, moths and dragonflies both wings are membranous. In some insects such as grasshoppers, the front pair, called tegmina, are quite tough and protect the delicate hind wings. In beetles the front pair, known as elytra, are very hard and tough and make the insect look as though it has no wings. The true flies have no hind wings, instead they have tiny pin-like structures which help to balance and stabilise the insect in flight. Title: How to Distinguish Between a Friend and a Lover Introduction: Determining whether someone is a friend or a lover can be challenging at times, especially when you have strong feelings towards them. While there are no definitive rules that apply to everyone, understanding the differences between friendship and romantic love can help guide your decision-making process. This tutorial will provide an in-depth explanation of various steps to assist you in distinguishing between a friend and a lover. Step 1: Analyze Your Feelings Examine your emotions carefully. Ask yourself if you feel attracted to this person physically, emotionally, or intellectually. If you experience butterflies, excitement, or heightened senses around them, these could be signs of romantic attraction. On the other hand, if your primary emotion is comfort, trust, and ease, these might indicate deep friendship. Key Tip: Remember, having both sets of feelings doesn't necessarily mean confusion; it just means you value different aspects of their presence in your life. Step 2: Assess The Nature Of Interactions Reflect upon the nature of your interactions with this individual. Are they more playful, flirtatious, or serious? Romantic relationships often involve physical touch like hugging, holding hands, or kissing. However, keep in mind that some friends may also engage in friendly touches such as pats on the back or high fives. Guideline: Pay attention to patterns over time rather than isolated incidents. Consistent romantic behaviors usually signal romantic interest. Step 3: Consider Their Importance To You Think about where this person fits into your priorities list. If they occupy a significant place and you find yourself willing to go out of your way frequently for them, then they likely hold great importance in your life. Determine whether this importance stems from friendship or romantic inclinations by asking yourself what specifically makes them so valuable to you. Step 4: Reflect On Shared Activities Consider the activities you do together. Lovers typically enjoy spending intimate time alone together doing couple-like things (e.g., going on dates, watching movies while cuddling). Friends, however, tend to spend time in group settings engaging in social activities. But remember, even couples hang out with mutual friends sometimes! Step 5: Understand The Role Of Intimacy Intimacy plays a crucial role in both friendships and romantic relationships but manifests differently. Emotional intimacy - sharing personal thoughts, fears, hopes - is common in both types of connections. Sexual/physical intimacy, though, is generally exclusive to lovers. Yet, note that certain close friendships may share non-sexual forms of physical affection too. Step 6: Evaluate Future Expectations Imagine potential future scenarios involving this person. Would you prefer growing old together, getting married, having children (if applicable)? Or would you rather see them succeed individually while maintaining a supportive bond? Depending on your answers, you might lean toward viewing this relationship as either romantic or platonic. Conclusion: Remember, labeling a relationship isn't always necessary. People can exist somewhere along the spectrum without clear definitions. By following these steps and being honest with yourself, you should gain better insight into whether someone is a friend or a lover for you. Ultimately, self-awareness and respect for others' feelings form the foundation of healthy interpersonal dynamics. She only talked me into one thing. It's going to be a huge big carrot. Title: How to Identify Fin Clues That Someone is Trustworthy Introduction: Trust is an essential aspect of any relationship, whether personal or professional. It can be challenging to determine if someone is trustworthy based solely on their words. However, observing their body language, including fin clues, can provide valuable insights. This tutorial will guide you through identifying fin clues that indicate someone is trustworthy. Step 1: Observe the Person's Posture When standing or sitting, a person who is trustworthy tends to have an open posture. Their shoulders are back, chest out, and arms uncrossed. This posture signals confidence and honesty. On the other hand, crossed arms or slouching may suggest defensiveness or lack of interest. Key Tip: Pay attention to the context. A person might cross their arms due to coldness or discomfort rather than dishonesty. Step 2: Look at Their Facial Expressions A trustworthy person usually has congruent facial expressions that match their words. For instance, they smile when talking about something happy or look serious when discussing something sad. Also, notice if their eyebrows move upwards as they talk, indicating genuine emotion. Guideline: Be aware that some people may naturally have less expressive faces (called a poker face) but still be trustworthy. Step 3: Check Eye Contact Eye contact is crucial in determining trustworthiness. A person who maintains appropriate eye contact during conversation shows attentiveness and sincerity. Avoidance of eye contact could imply nervousness, guilt, or deceitfulness. Key Tip: Remember cultural differences regarding eye contact norms. Some cultures consider prolonged eye contact rude. Step 4: Analyze Hand Gestures Hand gestures accompanying speech can reveal truthfulness. People who gesture openly while speaking - palms facing upward, hands moving freely - tend to be more credible. Conversely, limited hand movements or hiding hands under the table might signal concealment. Guideline: Keep in mind that certain medical conditions like Parkinson's disease can limit spontaneous movement, making it difficult for affected individuals to display natural hand gestures. Step 5: Listen for Consistency in Speech Listen carefully to what the person says. If their story remains consistent throughout the conversation without major discrepancies, this indicates reliability. Flip-flopping between different versions suggests unreliability. Step 6: Note Any Signs of Nervousness While occasional fidgeting doesn't necessarily mean untrustworthiness, excessive fiddling with objects, hair tugging, or nail biting could indicate anxiety or deception. Key Tip: Consider external factors causing stress before jumping to conclusions about dishonesty. Conclusion: By paying close attention to these fin clues, you can gain a better understanding of whether someone is likely to be trustworthy. However, no single clue guarantees trustworthiness; assess multiple signs together within the broader context. Moreover, remember that everyone exhibits subtle variations in body language influenced by personality traits, culture, mood, etc., so avoid oversimplifying interpretations. Title: How to Email MLS Documents to Yourself Emailing Multiple Listing Service (MLS) documents to yourself can be a convenient way to access important real estate information from any device with an internet connection. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step, providing clear explanations and useful tips along the way. **Step 1: Access your MLS system** First, log into your MLS account using your username and password. Make sure you are using a secure network to protect sensitive data. If you're unsure about your login credentials or need assistance, contact your MLS administrator. **Step 2: Locate the document(s)** Navigate through the MLS interface to find the property listing that contains the document(s) you want to email to yourself. Familiarize yourself with the layout so you can quickly locate this area in future sessions. **Step 3: Open the document(s)** Click on the specific document you wish to send to open it within your MLS platform. Review the content to ensure it is the correct file before proceeding. Keep in mind some MLS systems may have restrictions on certain types of files that can be emailed. **Step 4: Save the document(s) locally** Before sending the document as an attachment via email, save a copy to your computer or cloud storage service like Google Drive or Dropbox. Saving the document ensures you always have access to the most recent version even if changes occur after sending the initial email. To download the file, look for an icon resembling an arrow pointing downwards onto a line, usually located near the print or share options. **Key Tip**: Always double-check the saved filename to avoid confusion when organizing multiple documents. **Step 5: Compose a new email message** Open your preferred email client (e.g., Gmail, Outlook) and start composing a new message. Address it to yourself at your primary email address. Although this might seem unnecessary, doing so creates a backup of the document while also making it accessible across various devices. **Step 6: Attach the document(s)** Next, attach the previously saved document(s) by clicking on the paperclip icon or choosing 'Attach File' from the dropdown menu. Navigate to where you saved the document(s), select them, then hit 'Open'. Repeat this process for additional files if needed. **Step 7: Add relevant details** In the body of the email, include pertinent information related to the attached document(s). Explain why you sent these files to yourself and what actions should be taken upon receiving them. Providing context makes it easier for you to understand the purpose behind each attachment later on. **Step 8: Send the email** Once everything looks good, click 'Send.' The email, complete with the attached document(s), will now appear in your inbox. Check your spam folder just in case it gets filtered there accidentally. **Guidelines:** * Be cautious when forwarding confidential information. Remove any unneeded personal details before sharing with others. * Regularly clean out old emails containing sensitive documents to maintain security and organization. * Use encryption methods provided by your MLS or third-party services to further safeguard transmitted data. By following these steps and guidelines, you can effortlessly email MLS documents to yourself, ensuring easy accessibility and convenience whenever necessary. Do you play with it over his house too? Chess is a fascinating game. It originated very long ago and is played nowadays by millions of players. Almost everybody has played a few games in his or her life and knows about the basic rules of chess. In case you don't or want to refresh your basic knowledge, we offer you a small course about chess. Maybe you will be fascinated by the tactics and strategies involved and chess will become your greatest pleasure. The playing field consists of 64 squares, with 32 black and 32 white squares. It consists of 8 rows, labeled 1 to 8, and 8 columns labeled a to h. The rows are called ranks and the columns are called files. The squares are given names: a1, b1, c1, d1, e1, f1, g1 and h1 on the first row. b1. b2, b3, b4, b5, b6. b7 and b8 on the second row and so on.. The field s a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 and a8 form the a-file. h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, h7 and h8 is the h-file. These names are used to make it possible to explain where the pieces are standing. In order to record your games there is a special chess notation. In the notation the following symbols are used: K for King, Q for Queen, R for Rook, B for Bishop and the N for Knight (The K is already used for the King). For pawns no symbol are used. To describe a move first record the symbol for the piece, then the square it stands on, followed by the square it is going to. e.g. Qh3-e6. The Queen moves from h3 to e6. e2-e4. The pawn moves from e2 two squares ahead to square e4. Note that this is the long notation, there is also a shorter form in where only the destination square is given. The starting position Place the board in front of you and make sure the is a white square (h1) on your right hand. Place the white pieces on the first row on the board: The King on square e1, the Queen on d1, Rooks on a1 and h1, Knights on b1 and g1 and the Bishops on c1 and f1. Place the white pawns on the second row in front of the pieces on the squares a2 to h2. Place the black pieces symmetrical at the opposite site at the 8th row and the black pawns at the 7th row. - Observe the white Queen is on a white square, and the black Queen on a black square. This piece is the most important piece in the game. The King can move in any direction one square per move. For example: Kg4-g3, Kg3-f3 or Kf3-e4 - Observe that a King in the middle of an empty board can move to 8 squares The Queen is the most powerful piece. She can move horizontally, vertically and also diagonally, as many squares as she likes. For example: Qh5-a5. Qa5-c3 or Qc3-c6. - Note that a Queen in the middle of an empty board can reach 27 squares. The Rook can move horizontally or vertically as far as it likes. For example: Ra3-a6, Ra6-g6 or Rg6-g3 - Check that a Rook on an empty board can go to 14 squares. - Notice that in the starting position, the Rook is not allowed to move. The Bishop moves diagonally. It always remains on the same colored square. For example: Bf3-c6 or Bc6-a4. - Check that we have a white white squared Bishop, a white black squared Bishop, a black white squared Bishop, and a black black squared Bishop. The Knight moves in an L-shape. 2 squares horizontally and 1 vertically or 2 squares vertically and 1 horizontally. A Knight is not obstructed by own or opponent's pieces. It jumps over them. For example: Ng1-f3, Nf3-e5, Ne5-d4 - Check that a Knight in the middle of an empty board can move to 8 squares. - Notice that a Knight in the starting position is only looking to 3 squares. All Pieces can move forward and backward. None of the pieces except the knights can jump over other pieces standing in their way. The value of the pieces is considered to be: pawn 1, Rook 5, Knight 3, Bishop 3, Queen 9. A good player is expected to win if he is 2 points ahead in a favorable position. In general a pawn moves forward one square. But when a pawn has not yet moved, it may also move two squares forward. The pawn can go only forward. Not as the other pieces go backward. For example: d2-d4, d4-d5 and d5-d6. - Check that in the starting position White can choose between 16 pawn moves. If a pawn reaches the other side, the pawn promotes. The player can choose if it promotes to a Queen, Rook, Bishop or Knight. Logically mostly a queen is preferred. A pawn promotion is an important goal to play for. An important difference between a pawn and the other pieces is, that the moving is different from capturing. The pawn moves forward vertically, but capture diagonally forward. For example: Put a white pawn on b2 and a black pawn on c3. The white pawn can move to b3 or b4, or take the black pawn on c3. The 'En passant' (French for 'passing by') move is a special rule about a pawn capturing another pawn. If a pawn moves 2 squares forward and it stops next to an opponent's pawn on an adjacent line, then the opponent's pawn is allowed to take that pawn. It seems the pawn has moved only one square forward. This en passant taking is only allowed for the move, immediately following the previous pawn move.For example: put a black pawn on a4, and a white pawn on b2. The white pawn moves two squares ahead and goes to b4. Only now, the black pawn can take the white pawn. The white pawn is removed and the black pawn ends on b3. In notation: a4xb3 e.p. Attacking and capture If a piece can move to a square on which an opposite colored piece is standing, then the piece is attacking the opponent's piece. It is possible to move the first piece to the occupied square and remove the opposite colored piece from the board: the piece captures another piece. In the notation the symbol 'x' means capture.For example: If we have a white Rook on b3 and a black knight on b6. We can do the move Rb3xb6. The Rook moves from the square b3 and takes the piece on b6. A piece can not take a piece of its own color. In that case, the piece defends its own piece. Note: Capture is optional and not forced. In the starting position the pawns shield off the attacking possibilities of their own pieces. Check that in the starting position, not a single piece is attacked. Check and Checkmate When a piece attacks the opposite colored King, that King is in check. On the next move the king must be taken out of check. In notation the symbol '+' means check.For example: Ra1-a7+. The Rook moves from square a1 to a7 and gives check. There are three ways to move the king out of check: 1) Move the King to a safe square 2) Capture the attacking giving piece 3) Move a piece between the King and the attacker. The goal of the game is to checkmate the opponent's King. When a King is in check and connot be taken out of check, the King is in checkmate and the game has ended. Can you find the check possibility for White after the starting moves 1) e2-e4 f7-f5 ? Can you find the mate possibility for Black after 1) f2-f4 e7-e6 2) g2-g4 ? This mate is called Fool's mate, and is the shortest mate possible. This is the only move when two pieces are moving: the King and the Rook. The king moves two (normally 1) squares to the Rook, and the Rook is placed at the other side (jumping) of the King.There are two kinds of castling: long castling and short castling. Often both players castle in a game. It puts the King in a safe(r) position, and it activates the Rook.For example: Start from the initial position: 1) d2-d4, e7-e6 2) Nb1-c3 Ng8-f6 3) Bc1-g5 Bf8-e7 4) Qd1-d2. Now Black is allowed to short castling: 4) ... 0-0. The King goes to square g8. The rook to square f8. Now White is allowed to long castling: 5) 0-0-0. The King goes to square c1 and the Rook to square d1. Castling is only allowed when: a) The King and the specific Rook have not moved. b) The squares between the King and the specific Rook are empty. c) The King is not in check (on e1). d) None of the squares the King, passes (on d1 or f1), are attacked. e) The King does not end in check (on c1 or g1). Always White starts moving 1 piece, then Black on piece, then White, then Black, etc. until the game ends. Note that a move has to be made. There is no "pass" possibility. - Check that in the starting position White can choose between 20 moves. - Notice that White after having played the Kings pawn e2-e4, he can choose between 15 pawn moves + 4 Queen moves + 5 Bishop moves = 24 moves. win or draw The goal of the game is to checkmate the other King. When white checkmates the black King, White wins the game: 1-0. When black checkmates the white King, Black wins: 0-1 When neither site is able to checkmate the other King, the result is a draw: 1/2 -1/2 A draw can be reached in different ways: A player is not in check, but is also not able to move. - No mating potential. There is insufficient material to checkmate the other King. A King and Knight or King and Bishop, can not checkmate the other King. Note that a pawn can often promote to queen. - Perpetual check. A player is able to continuously give check. The motive can be a worse position, or the inability to continue the attack. - Threefold repetition. The same position is reached for the third time with the same player moving and with the same moving possibilities. - 50 move rule. If during 50 moves no pawn has moved AND no pieces have been captured, a draw can be claimed. - Draw agreed. In more complicated positions, after having done a move, a player can propose a draw. The opponent may accept or decline this offer. As mentioned before, the goal of the game is to checkmate the opponents king. It obvious this goal not not be reached directly. Therefore there are some subgoals in the different phases of the game. In this phase the goal is to develop your pieces. In this phase you will de strengtening your position in order to prepare the attack on the opponents king. Now it time to go for the king. Ready to play You have just learned all the rules for playing chess and now you are ready to play a game of chess! We hope you enjoyed this course and that the explanations were clear enough for you. If you want to know more about some basic strategies in chess, you will notice your understanding will improve by playing, reading books, visit a chess club, play internet chess and learning with a chess program. Title: How to Change Your Google Sign-in on Android Google sign-in is a crucial aspect of many Android devices as it allows users to access various services such as Gmail, Play Store, Drive, and more with just one account. However, there might be situations where you need to change your Google sign-in, like when sharing a device or switching accounts. This tutorial will guide you through changing your Google sign-in on an Android device in detail, emphasizing clarity, practicality, and easy understanding. **Key Tips**: Always remember your current Google password and have your new account details ready before proceeding. Also, note that changing the Google sign-in will affect all synced data and settings associated with the previous account. Make sure to back up any important information if necessary. ### Step 1: Access Settings Navigate to your home screen or app drawer and locate the **Settings** app (gear icon). Tap on it to open the settings menu. The settings allow you to customize various aspects of your device, including user accounts. ### Step 2: Go to Accounts Section Scroll down until you find the **"Accounts"** option (usually located under 'Personal' or 'Privacy'). It may display a small person icon or simply say "Users & accounts." Once found, tap on this section. ### Step 3: Manage Google Account Under the "Accounts" section, select the **"Google"** option to manage your existing Google accounts linked to the device. If multiple Google accounts are added, choose the specific one you want to modify from the list below. ### Step 4: Remove Existing Google Account Tap the three vertical dots at the upper right corner or press "Menu" button to reveal additional options. Select **"Remove account,"** which disconnects your Google account from the device. Confirm removal by tapping **"OK"** or **"Remove account"**. You can now add a new Google sign-in. ### Step 5: Add New Google Account Return to the main **"Accounts"** page and select **"Add account"**, then **"Google"**. Follow the prompts carefully, inputting your email address and password associated with the new Google account. After accepting terms and conditions, complete the verification process via phone call or text message. Finally, give permissions according to your preferences during setup. ### Step 6: Verify Successful Switch To ensure successful completion, check whether the new Google account appears under the **"Accounts"** section. Additionally, launch apps relying on Google sync functionality, such as Gmail or Play Store, and verify if they reflect changes regarding the newly signed-in account. By following these steps meticulously, you should successfully change your Google sign-in on your Android device without encountering significant issues. Remember to keep track of both old and new credentials while also considering potential impacts on synchronized content across platforms. Exploring Layers in the Mind and Living Beyond Them Introduction: The human mind is a complex and multifaceted entity, capable of processing vast amounts of information and experiencing a wide range of emotions. One way to understand the workings of the mind is by examining its layers. By exploring these layers, we can gain insight into our thoughts, feelings, and behaviors, and learn to transcend limiting beliefs and patterns. This tutorial will provide a comprehensive guide on how to explore the layers of the mind and live beyond them. Step 1: Understanding the Conscious Mind The conscious mind is the part of the mind that is aware of the present moment and actively engaged in thought and decision-making. It is responsible for logical thinking, problem-solving, and short-term memory. To explore this layer of the mind, start by observing your thoughts and paying attention to what you are focusing on in any given moment. Key Tips: * Practice mindfulness meditation to increase awareness of the present moment * Keep a journal of your thoughts and reflect on recurring themes or patterns * Engage in activities that challenge your intellect and require focused attention Step 2: Uncovering the Subconscious Mind The subconscious mind is the part of the mind that operates below the level of consciousness and stores memories, habits, and automatic responses. It is responsible for controlling bodily functions, regulating emotions, and storing long-term memories. To explore this layer of the mind, try techniques such as hypnosis, guided imagery, or visualization. Key Tips: * Identify and challenge limiting beliefs or negative self-talk * Use positive affirmations to reprogram the subconscious mind with empowering messages * Develop a regular practice of relaxation or stress-reduction techniques to access the subconscious mind more easily Step 3: Accessing the Collective Unconscious The collective unconscious is the deepest layer of the mind, shared by all humans and containing universal archetypes and symbols. It is responsible for intuition, creativity, and spiritual connection. To explore this layer of the mind, engage in practices that promote introspection, imagination, and symbolic interpretation. Key Tips: * Study mythology, dreams, art, and literature to uncover common themes and motifs * Cultivate a sense of wonder and curiosity about the world around you * Practice creative expression through writing, drawing, painting, music, or dance Step 4: Transcending the Ego The ego is the aspect of the mind that defines the self and creates a sense of separation from others. While necessary for survival and individual identity, the ego can also create limitations and barriers to growth and fulfillment. To transcend the ego and live beyond the layers of the mind, cultivate qualities such as compassion, empathy, and interconnectedness. Key Tips: * Practice gratitude and appreciation for the present moment * Connect with nature and other living beings * Let go of judgment and criticism towards yourself and others Conclusion: By exploring the layers of the mind and developing a deeper understanding of their function and purpose, we can begin to live beyond them and tap into our full potential as human beings. Through mindfulness, introspection, and transcendence, we can overcome limiting beliefs, connect with others, and experience life in a richer and more meaningful way. Remember, the journey to self-discovery is ongoing and requires patience, persistence, and an open mind. Fixed-wing aircraft allow people to travel longer distances, and faster than ships or trains. Aircraft can fly from New York to London in about 7 hours. It would take one week or more on a ship. Militaries use airplanes to carry their soldiers quickly from place to place. But what have you been doing, Pant?" "What Satan always does," Pant smiled strangely. |A wedge is a simple machine shaped like an inclined plane. A wedge is actually like a moving inclined plane. An easy way to see how a wedge works is to think of it as an inclined plane standing on its narrow end. A fairly weak force, applied to the wide end of a wedge whose narrow end is being pushed into something, will send a strong force pushing out at the sides. An example would be to take a wedge of steel and bang it into the end of a log, the log will split open. We can use the wedge action to cut and shape ice and wood sculptures, clay or whatever. A wedge may seem like a simple tool. But the wedge is very important while doing work, crafts, or at play. Yes, even play! A shovel is acting as a wedge while you're shoveling the sand or snow that you're playing in. I think you'll agree that it's a lot easier to move an object using a wedge than if it were moved by only your hands. TRY THIS EXPERIMENT To see how a wedge works, hold the point of a nail on top of a piece of thick cardboard. Press the nail down against the cardboard. The point enters the cardboard first and makes a path for the larger part of the nail to enter and separate the paper. ON YOUR OWN Make a list of different wedges you have around your home or in your classroom. Wedge - You can use a wedge to split a block of wood, by driving it into the wood with a hammer.
